properties.description: AKASA is a private software development company providing generative AI solutions for healthcare revenue cycle management, including prior authorization and claim management; 118 employees, $7M annual revenue, $205M funding, founded 2019 in South San Francisco, CA. Recognized for real-world GenAI deployment at Cleveland Clinic, processing tens of thousands of healthcare encounters, and operating under HIPAA, HITRUST, and SOC 2 compliance.
properties.content:
# Transforming your revenue cycle with generative AI

Reducing denials. Improving margins. Increasing revenue. Advanced generative AI (GenAI) trained on clinical and financial data can help make it a reality. Give your health system revenue cycle staff the superpowers they desperately need.

[LET'S CHAT](https://akasa.com/request-a-demo)

# A revenue cycle that's faster, smarter, and better

AKASA is leveraging our deep RCM expertise and the best technology available to improve the revenue cycle. We’re using GenAI, trained on clinical and financial data, to power our platform and solutions. The results? Faster speed-to-value, decreased cost-to-collect, empowered teams, and greater patient satisfaction.

AKASA is the leading provider of generative AI solutions for the healthcare revenue cycle

#### Prebill Optimization Suite

Unify coding and CDI to close documentation gaps, ensure accuracy, and improve quality with GenAI-powered optimization.

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/optimization/)

#### Coding Optimizer

Surface missed coding opportunities, uncover quality and compliance risks, and boost revenue integrity with GenAI insights.

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/coding-optimizer/)

#### CDI Optimizer

Uncover documentation gaps and enable accurate patient documentation with a GenAI assistant

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/cdi-optimizer/)

#### AI Advisor

Find answers, documents, and context faster with a research assistant built for revenue cycle teams

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/ai-advisor/)

#### Auth Status

Check authorization status automatically, reducing manual follow-ups

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/auth-status/)

#### Claim Status

Obtain up-to-date status information for claims automatically, alleviating the burden on your staff

[EXPLORE](https://akasa.com/solutions/claim-status/)

# GenAI for RCM

ChatGPT is everywhere. Have you ever thought about how you could use it for the revenue cycle? At AKASA, we have.

We’ve improved the generative AI that powers ChatGPT by optimizing it for healthcare and putting it directly into staff workflows. Our AI knows revenue cycle, grasps the nuances of your health system, understands clinical context, and helps you do more.

[HOW OUR PLATFORM WORKS](https://akasa.com/platform/)

### AKASA by the numbers

company.name: AKASA
company.about:
AKASA is rapidly becoming the GenAI partner of choice for leading health systems, with recent national recognition of our Cleveland Clinic deployment as “one of the most comprehensive real-world uses of GenAI in healthcare finance to date.” 

Across customers, AKASA now delivers an average of $3.1M in additional revenue and 4,270 additional quality indicators per 10,000 discharges, demonstrating consistent, measurable impact.

Our advantage comes from building a custom LLM for each health system, capturing their clinical nuance to drive superior accuracy across the mid-cycle — spanning prior authorization, CDI, inpatient coding, and clinical denials appeals. This approach allows organizations to modernize critical workflows and unlock material improvements in both revenue and quality.

With investors including Andreessen Horowitz, BOND, Costanoa Ventures, and others, we’ve raised more than $200M to help eliminate the financial complexity of healthcare.

We’re rebuilding the revenue cycle with AI. Let’s chat.

references.title: Cleveland Clinic and AKASA Deepen AI Partnership To Modernize the Revenue Cycle - Press - Akasa
references.snippet:
South San Francisco, Calif. — October 13, 2025 — AKASA today announced an expanded partnership with Cleveland Clinic focused on the mid-revenue cycle. The health system was recently the first organization to complete a pilot of the company’s clinical documentation integrity (CDI) solution and will now implement it across all its U.S. locations.
[...]
Earlier this year, Cleveland Clinic completed the rollout of the AKASA AI coding tool, implementing it in all its U.S. locations over the course of four months and using it to process tens of thousands of encounters already.
[...]
Building on that collaboration, the new CDI AI deployment brings the same approach to documentation improvement through generative AI (GenAI). CDI represents an even more complex task than coding. CDI staff need to review all the documentation that a coder reviews, in addition to labs, imaging vitals, medications, and other complex multi-modal data. This tool allows CDI staff to leverage an AI assistant to support the accuracy and efficiency of their work.
[...]
The CDI solution is part of a unified platform that connects documentation and coding into one workflow. The system is customized for each health system’s clinical and operational nuances and designed with an interface that surfaces key clinical evidence directly to staff, making it easier to prioritize their time.
[...]
"Integrating advanced AI solutions into our revenue cycle is a testament to Cleveland Clinic's commitment to innovation and operational excellence,” said Ben Shahshahani, Ph.D., Chief AI Officer at Cleveland Clinic. “The success of the AI coding platform has demonstrated the transformative impact of technology in enhancing efficiency and quality. With the launch of the CDI solution, we are excited to continue setting new benchmarks for how AI can support our teams, improve processes, and ultimately benefit our patients.” Cleveland Clinic, Duke University Health System, and AKASA will share their experiences on a panel at the AHIMA 2025 Conference, titled “Rewriting the Mid-Cycle: GenAI’s Role in Protecting Margins and Elevating Revenue Integrity” (Monday, October 13 at 1:45 p.m.), to share real-world lessons and challenges from bringing GenAI into the mid-cycle.
[...]
AKASA is the leading provider of generative AI (GenAI) for healthcare revenue cycle management. It uses the latest technology to help leading health systems and academic medical centers get credit for 100% of the services they provide, producing better financial and quality outcomes while delivering more precise care. Powered by the AKASA Platform, our technology combines deep revenue cycle knowledge with custom large language models (LLMs) trained on a health system’s own clinical and financial data. Recognized by Black Book Market Research as the #1 healthcare RCM startup, AKASA helps organizations address complex workflows and capture the full value of care to deliver accuracy, compliance, and efficiency at scale.

references.snippet:
How AKASA Handles Data | Platform | AKASA

80% of health systems are taking action on GenAI in the revenue cycle. READ THE NEW REPORT.

### platform | trust & security

# The protection of your data is our priority

###### We understand your concern and caution about sharing sensitive data. As a HIPAA-compliant, HITRUST-certified, and SOC 2-certified company, we protect your data at all costs.

REQUEST DEMO

Overview

Benefits

Features

## Privacy and security compliance

Tools and automation requires data. There’s no getting around that. But, unlike many other vendors in the revenue cycle technology space, we don't require any on-premise installations and have multiple options to exchange data securely. Our HIPAA-compliant security infrastructure is built on a foundation of the highest industry standards, including HITRUST CSF, SOC 2, NIST-800-53, and CIS. Our dedicated IT team does the heavy lifting and takes any security concerns or risks off your plate. In the event of a flagged security concern during our 24/7 monitoring, we’ve developed a detailed Incident Response Policy to keep your data and organization safe.

## Safety measures that matter

#### Encryption

We employ ZeroTrust VPN and FIPS 140-2 compliant data-at-rest and data-in-transit encryption protocols and keys, which are employed with complete user lockdown and privilege controls.

#### Benchmarking

We utilize CIS Level 1 hardening standards and Industry Best Practices within our AWS infrastructure. CIS benchmarks are accepted in business, industry, and government use, and require strict adherence to safety.

#### Access auditing

Any access to PHI data is logged with detailed information. In the event of a suspected breach, we have documented security protocols we quickly put into place to keep information secure.

#### 24/7 monitoring

We monitor our automation 24/7, with strict access monitoring metrics established. Any deviation for our automation or relating to access immediately triggers our alerts and response protocols.

## In-depth defense and layered security

Many tiered security groups

Network and host-based firewalls

Network and sub-network isolation

Multi-factor authentication

A low attack surface

## AKASA achieves SOC 2 type 2 certification

## Everything as code

Infrastructure

Accounts and payments

Auditing and compliance

Product documentation and defenses

New tax year, new £20,000 ISA allowance

# See your savings grow tax free with our 2 year Fixed Rate ISA

4.40% AER/tax-free p.a. (fixed). 1 year Fixed Rate option and Investment ISAs also available.

Limits and criteria apply. Early closure charges may apply and interest is paid annually and at maturity. Limited availability, can be withdrawn at any time.

### Explore our products and services

#### Our products

#### Help and Support

[Bank of England base rate changes](https://natwest.com/mortgages/bank-of-england-base-rate.html)

[Get a Financial Health-check](https://natwest.com/financial-health-check.html)

[Ways to bank with us](https://www.natwest.com/banking-with-natwest.html?intcam=PC_HP-P4-1-ALL-DEF)

[Our commitment to accessibility](https://natwest.com/accessibility.html)

## You could win £100,000 when you invest with NatWest

For every £50 you invest in a NatWest Stocks and Shares ISA, you’ll get one entry into the prize draw. The more you invest, the more chances you have to win!

For a chance to win first prize of £100,000, 50 prizes of £1,000 or 1,000 of £100, enter between 12th January and 30th April 2026.

When investing your capital is at risk. T&Cs, fees and charges apply.

### Join millions on our Mobile App

Did you know that over 10 million customers now use the NatWest Mobile App to access their account every day? Take a look at some of our handy features below.

![](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy_850418814/whatineedtoknowimage_0.coreimg.jpeg/1757424151494/nw-pers-insights-mobile-app.jpeg)

### Spending Insights

Take control of your finances with tips and personalised coaching plans to help you manage your financial well being. Eligibility criteria may apply.

![](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy_850418814/whatineedtoknowimage_1.coreimg.jpeg/1728399895942/nw-pers-payments-hub.jpeg)

### Payments Hub

Our Payments hub brings payments to the front and centre of our app. Send, request and manage your money all at the tap of a button. Eligibility criteria and limits apply.

![](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy_850418814/whatineedtoknowimage_2.coreimg.jpeg/1728399903511/nw-pers-credit-score.jpeg)

### Credit Score

Check your free credit score in the app today, so you know where you stand. Credit score is available once you opt in and provided by TransUnion. Eligibility criteria apply.

![](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy_850418814/whatineedtoknowimage_3.coreimg.jpeg/1728399967938/nw-pers-round-ups.jpeg)

### Round Ups

Watch the pennies add up with Round Ups. Round up your spending to the nearest pound, sending your change to your savings account. Eligibility criteria applies.

![](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy_850418814/whatineedtoknowimage_4.coreimg.jpeg/1728399934266/nw-pers-favourite-payees.jpeg)

### Favourite Payees

Are there people that you pay again and again? Save them to your favourite payees so you can send money faster. Add a picture to identify them too.

Our app is available to personal and business banking customers aged 11+ using compatible iOS and Android devices. You'll need a UK or international mobile number in specific countries.

### More from NatWest

![Image of Max Whitlock](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy/whatineedtoknowimage_0.coreimg.jpeg/1771500524971/nw-team-gb-max-hero.jpeg)

#### We're proud to partner with Team GB

We aim to inspire success with our partners and help the nation take action. Learn how this partnership benefits our customers, and our communities.

![Woman working out household sums](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy/whatineedtoknowimage_1.coreimg.jpeg/1771500601520/nw-photo-cost-of-living-laptop-getty-hero.jpeg)

### Help with the cost of living

The cost of living has increased in recent years due to inflation, energy prices, tax rises, and other factors. While everyone feels the pinch, we offer practical tools to help.

![Financial Services Compensation Scheme protection logo](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_309020852/whatineedtoknow_copy/whatineedtoknowimage_2.coreimg.jpeg/1776679799311/logo-web-purple-fscs-protected.jpeg)

### New FSCS Limit of £120,000

The Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) limit has increased to £120,000. This means your eligible deposits with us are protected up to a total of £120,000 by the FSCS, the UK's deposit guarantee scheme.

![Graph showing independent service quality results for personal current accounts. Natwest are ranked 11th.](https://natwest.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1914176944/singlearticlev2_copy/article_image.coreimg.png/1771234013930/nwpcagbwebpng.png)

Graph showing independent service quality results for personal current accounts. Natwest are ranked 13th.

### Independent service quality survey results for personal current accounts

As part of a regulatory requirement, an independent survey was conducted to ask customers of the largest 17 personal current account providers if they would recommend their provider to friends or family.

### Authorised push payment (APP) scams rankings in 2024

Authorised push payment (APP) scams happen when someone is tricked into transferring money to a fraudster’s bank account. Information about NatWest's performance prior to the introduction of the reimbursement requirement in October 2024 can be found in [PSR's latest APP Scams Performance Report](https://www.psr.org.uk/media/b50p3mlm/psr-2024-app-scam-performance-data-february-2026.pdf) published in February 2026.

company.name: NatWest
company.about:
We believe in taking responsibility, and that we all have a part to play in creating the world we want.

That’s why we’re empowering our 14 million personal, small business and SME customers in England and Wales, making it easier to control their money when and how they want to. From our branch network to our 24 hour contact centres, and from customer AI to our multi award winning app, we’re making banking simple and secure. And we’re making the most of our data, using what we know to be proactive and help our customers through the moments that shape their lives.

We’re supporting the UK economy by lending more to British businesses than any other bank. And through our Entrepreneur Accelerator hubs around the country, we’re standing behind the drivers of tomorrow’s growth.

Our culture is defined by opportunity and inclusion. We want all our people to be able to be their full selves at work, and to take control of their career destiny. So we’re providing great training, making it easy to learn, develop and progress, whatever your background.

If you’re engaged, proactive, and customer led, there’s a place for you with us.

evaluations.reasoning: NatWest is a UK banking institution and, as such, is subject to data‑handling regulations such as GDPR and PCI DSS.  The company’s own website contains a dedicated GDPR compliance guide and a PCI DSS compliance page, confirming that it must meet these regulatory requirements.  These obligations directly influence how the bank can deploy cloud services and AI solutions, as they must ensure data protection, encryption, and secure processing of cardholder data.  Therefore NatWest satisfies the criterion of operating under regulatory data‑handling obligations that affect cloud and AI deployment choices.

result: NatWest publicly documents production AI inference usage, deploying nearly 100 ML models on Amazon SageMaker with a goal of thousands more in the next two years. The Cora chatbot handled about 11 million customer conversations in 2023, signaling large-scale AI interactions, and the bank references over 100 use cases across the organization. Public spend on annualized inference isn’t disclosed; challenges cited include cost and capacity in large-scale ML deployments. (Sources: AWS NatWest case study: https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/case-studies/natwest/; Harnessing AI in banking: https://www.natwest.com/corporates/insights/technology/harnessing-ai-in-banking.html)
enrichments.reasoning: Based on publicly documented NatWest AI deployment details. The AWS case study states NatWest has deployed nearly 100 machine learning models on Amazon SageMaker and aims to scale to thousands more within the next two years, while NatWest’s AI article notes the Cora chatbot handled about 11 million conversations in 2023 and references over 100 use cases. There are no public figures for annualized inference spend, and typical pain points include cost and capacity in large-scale ML deployments. Inline sources are provided for verification.

# How NatWest Bank Personalizes the Customer Experience Using AWS

## How NatWest Bank Personalizes Customer Experience with AWS | Amazon Web Services

NatWest Group is one of the largest banks in the United Kingdom. Formally established in 1968, the company has origins dating back to 1727. NatWest Group seeks to use its rich legacy data to innovate and personalize its personal, business, and corporate banking and insurance services. Zackary Anderson, the chief data and analytics officer, discussed the company's use of machine learning and data analytics to engage and personalize messaging for its large customer base of 20 million customers. NatWest Group has successfully deployed nearly 100 machine learning models, using Amazon SageMaker, with the goal of having thousands in the next two years. The impact of its personalized messaging approach has been significant, including saving customers in poorer neighborhoods nearly half a million pounds in ATM fees within six months, reducing fraud, and enhancing overall customer wellbeing.

How NatWest Bank Personalizes Customer Experience with AWS

Play

## AWS Services Used

### Amazon SageMaker

Amazon SageMaker is built on Amazon’s two decades of experience developing real-world ML applications, including product recommendations, personalization, intelligent shopping, robotics, and voice-assisted devices.

[...]
By harnessing the power of data and AI we believe we can deliver a more personalised, relevant and safer banking experience for our customers. Our chatbot Cora handled c. 11 million retail customers conversations in 2023 alone. AI will further enable us to understand the most important conversations to have with customers, and when to have them. We can then tailor our products and services to meet their needs – whether that’s for buying a home, saving for the future, setting up and growing a business, or simply managing day-to-day finances.
[...]
For example, using AI, we identified in 2023, more than 1.4 million customers who were facing significant challenges associated with rising food and fuel costs. As a result, we were able to reach out to a proportion of them with supportive messages. AI also enables us, and our customers, to understand how they’re spending, so we can help them budget, save money and improve their financial resilience – like using the new ‘Insights’ feature on our mobile app.
[...]
A key priority is to keep our customers safe and secure. We already use AI to predict patterns and trends in financial crime and fraud and we continue to invest in the latest fraud prevention and detection technology. We’ve got almost 6,000 colleagues and specialists using AI, identifying key trends so we can educate and protect our customers, especially those who are at the greatest risk of falling victim to a crime. We’ve also made AI training available to colleagues.
[...]
In much the same way that AI is already embedded in many of our day-to-day lives at home, we’ve been using Al and data to enhance our customers’ experiences and the way we work for years. In fact, around 15% of the Bank’s analytical models already use traditional AI. It’s become synonymous with increased efficiency and productivity.
[...]
Through automation and augmentation, we’re being more efficient, freeing up more time to do the things that bring value to our customers. We’re investing in cloud data platforms, machine learning capabilities and data specialist resources to help us to take AI optimisation of our operations to the next level.
[...]
We’re well placed to scale and leverage our investment in cloud technologies and increase engineering skills and capabilities. We have created strong foundations to accelerate the roll out of AI across the bank.

We will provide you with all public information regarding the event and a link to our live stream on our website.Learn more](https://commerzbank.com/group/what-drives-us/fostering-success/2026-annual-general-meeting.html)

## Latest Press Releases

#### Commerzbank Rejects UniCredit’s Hostile Approach

#### Invitation to the Annual General Meeting of Commerzbank on 20 May 2026

#### Commerzbank reaffirms upside potential of existing business strategy

- [04/20/2026 - The Bank reaffirms the superior value of its standalone strategy with limited execution risk. Updated financial targets will be presented with Q1 results on 8 May.Read more](https://commerzbank.com/group/newsroom/press-releases/20260420-commerzbank-rejects-hostile-approach.html)
- [04/09/2026 - On the agenda are the decision on the dividend proposal of €1.10 per share and the vote on authorisation for further share buybacks.Read more](https://commerzbank.com/group/newsroom/press-releases/invitation-general-meeting.html)
- [04/07/2026 - Commerzbank currently sees no basis for a mutually agreed value-accretive transaction after interactions with UniCredit.Read more](https://commerzbank.com/group/newsroom/press-releases/commerzbank-reaffirms-business-strategy.html)

All press releases

## Careers

© Commerzbank

### It's time for an employer that suits you.

Live, work, have fun: Finding the right path can be so easy. Let’s move forward together. We offer you secure prospects, encourage bold ideas and reward your commitment. And we believe that the best solutions are created through diversity and satisfied employees.

Learn more

## Who we are

### Our Bank

Commerzbank is the leading bank for the German Mittelstand and a strong partner for around 24,000 corporate client groups. In addition, it supports private and small-business customers in Germany with more than €400 billion assets under management. As part of its international business, the Bank supports clients with German connectivity and companies operating in selected future-oriented industries. In the Private and Small-Business Customers segment, the Bank is at the side of its customers with its brands Commerzbank and comdirect: online and mobile, in the advisory centre, and personally in its branches. We are the Bank at the side of our customers. Your success is our business. The most important information, such as facts and figures on Commerzbank, strategy, and brand and management, can be found in the section "Who we are".

© Getty Images, pixelfit

© Commerzbank

### Our Strategy and brand

The Commerzbank is already the best Commerzbank there has ever been. With our further developed strategy "Momentum," we are now shifting up a gear: We will create even more value – for all our stakeholders: customers, investors, and employees. Our strategy is supported by a modern, dynamic, and distinctive brand presence. It highlights our high standards and reflects our identity. "Creating perspectives, shaping the future together" lies at the heart of the Commerzbank brand.

[Learn more about Commerzbank's strategy and brand](https://commerzbank.com/group/who-we-are/strategy-brand/)

Learn more

## Newsroom

[Information on Commerzbank's newsroom](https://commerzbank.com/group/newsroom/)

#### Contacts for journalists

#### Media Centre

## Commerzbank Research

Our leading indicator for the German economy and macroeconomic forecasts

Current short analyses from Commerzbank Research

Analyses and background on the economy, capital markets and finance. New every week.

#### Chief Economist

Current media appearances and comments by Dr Jörg Krämer

Research overview

## Service Topics

#### Contacts

We have an open ear for your concerns - whether you are a customer or not.

#### Price Information

Up-to-date, fast, and complete information provide the basis for successful investment decisions.

[Overview of price information](https://commerzbank.com/group/service/price-information/)

#### Overview for Suppliers

Commerzbank procures a wide range of goods and services to become the best bank for its clients.

Any further questions or suggestions?

Feel free to contact us!

company.name: Commerzbank AG
company.about: Commerzbank is the leading bank for the German Mittelstand and a strong partner for around 24,000 corporate client groups. In addition, it supports private and small-business customers in Germany with more than €400 billion assets under management. The Bank’s two Business Segments – Private and Small-Business Customers and Corporate Clients – offer a comprehensive portfolio of financial services. Commerzbank transacts approximately 30% of Germany’s foreign trade and is present internationally in more than 40 countries in the corporate clients’ business. The Bank focusses on the German Mittelstand, large corporates, and institutional clients. As part of its international business, Commerzbank supports clients with a business relationship to Germany, Austria, or Switzerland and companies operating in selected future-oriented industries. In the Private and Small-Business Customers segment, the Bank is at the side of its customers with its brands Commerzbank and comdirect: online and mobile, in the advisory centre, and personally in its branches. Its Polish subsidiary mBank S.A. is an innovative digital bank that serves approximately 5.7 million private and corporate customers, predominantly in Poland, as well as in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

references.title: Commerzbank Optimizes Anti-Money Laundering Measures through AI Partnership with Hawk
references.snippet:
Commerzbank Optimizes Anti-Money Laundering Measures through AI Partnership with Hawk

# Commerzbank Optimizes Anti-Money Laundering Measures through AI Partnership with Hawk

Commerzbank and Hawk, a provider of anti-fraud and anti-money laundering (AML) technologies, are collaborating to optimize internal banking processes using Artificial Intelligence (AI). The “AML AI Extended Risk Model” solution supplements the bank's existing rule-based compliance systems to significantly increase effectiveness in combating financial crime. This partnership underscores Commerzbank's strategy of deploying AI purposefully for customer and market processes while adapting to specific regulatory requirements.

“Given the complexity of the landscape, we can only successfully combat financial crime with the help of AI. It is a high strategic priority for us to proactively and continuously expand our compliance system architecture,” says Viktor Kraus, Cluster Lead Global Financial Crime Prevention Platform at Commerzbank.

Tobias Schweiger, CEO of Hawk, emphasizes: “Banks must adapt to new threat scenarios in money laundering. Our AI-driven solution helps achieve this.”

By using Hawk's extended risk model solution, banks benefit from complex AI models without the need for time-consuming updates to their existing technology. The software integrates seamlessly with legacy systems via an integration layer. Through this implementation, Commerzbank has:

- Increased the accuracy (targeting) of alerts and reduced false positives
- Detected more novel cases of money laundering or fraud
- Expanded software validation to include robust AI model governance

“Our software enables compliance teams to improve the quality and transparency of money laundering detection and investigations,” says Tobias Schweiger. “In this context, the explainable nature of our AI plays a central role – particularly regarding regulatory approval.”

Hans-Georg Beyer, Group Chief Compliance Officer at Commerzbank, adds: “Commerzbank is using its position as a pioneer to conduct its fight against money laundering in an even more targeted manner. The collaboration with Hawk is a vital step in this direction.”

10. March 2026

---

Share this page

references.snippet:
Data protection - Information Security - Commerzbank Data protection - Information Security - Commerzbank - Commerzbank

# Data Protection & Information Security

How we implement customer data protection and information security in the Group

## Data Protection

In times of increasing digitalisation, the protection of customer data and the preservation of banking secrecy are of utmost importance and a basic prerequisite for the satisfaction of our customers. That is why data security is a key issue for us in terms of responsible banking. Commerzbank AG takes the protection of your personal data very seriously and adheres to the rules of data protection, in particular the provisions of the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and our Group Data Protection Policy.

To the Data Protection Notification

## Your contact for queries regarding data protection

Data Protection Officer

## Responding to cybercrime

With the increasing digital networking of the state, the economy and society, cyber security and resilience are becoming ever more important. In this context, Commerzbank considers not only its own information, premises and IT systems, but also those of customers and service providers, including independent transport routes where applicable. Cyber security is a strong driver of trust and therefore also an important competitive factor. As part of the critical infrastructure, the financial industry is subject to extended legal requirements.

## Group Risk Management

We want to take appropriate account of this development and future challenges in managing cyber risks. Cyber and information security risks are managed by the Group Risk Management - Cyber Risk & Information Security (GRM-CRIS) unit. The area is under the responsibility of the Group Chief Information Security Officer. In addition to already established security functions such as the information security management system, the focus is on cyber risk management and strengthening Commerzbank's cyber resilience, including information security incident management capabilities.

## Related Topics

ESG Risk Management

How Commerzbank manages ESG Risk

Customer satisfaction

Living transparency and fairness in customer relationships

Human Rights and Environmental Protection

How we respect human rights and the environment in our supply chain.

# NatWest Group

## Annual Results 2025

![Flying banker NatWest colleague with customer in Orkney Islands](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_985479040_/singlearticlev2/article_image.coreimg.png/1770737459129/nwg-island-communities-1500x998.png)

## Succeeding with customers

Visit our Annual Report hub for more on how we’re making progress on our strategic priorities and becoming a vital and trusted partner to our customers.

![CEO Paul Thwaite at a manufacturing customer’s premises](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_85336660_c/singlearticlev2/article_image.coreimg.png/1771260413212/nwg-paul-thwaite-867x398.png)

## NatWest Group Annual Results 2025

Our CEO Paul Thwaite updates on our 2025 performance and financials, strategy and how we’re accelerating from a position of strength.

![this images shows two men walking across a factory floor ](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-two-men-on-factory-floor-DMM?qlt=82&ts=1770020905495&dpr=off)

## Growing Together: Our plan to support UK growth

NatWest Group is helping to build the conditions and connections for growth across all nations and regions of the UK.

Our Growing Together plan is a long-term, connected programme underpinned by five focus areas where we deliver impact today and can do even more in the future.

![This image shows two people sat down talking together. ](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-ARA-Hero-DMM?qlt=82&ts=1771261423129&dpr=off)

## Succeeding with customers through tech, data and AI

As technology reinvents how people live, work and bank, we’ve been transforming our tech and data foundations at NatWest Group and put in place leading capabilities – giving us the building blocks to meet our customers’ evolving needs.

## Latest news and insights

 

![](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1038915034/productlistingmultip_1880255377/product-listing-multiple-product/product_0/productimage.coreimg.png/1770798203066/nwg-evelyn-496x330.png)

### Acquisition of Evelyn Partners

An exciting opportunity to create the UK’s leading Private Banking and Wealth Management business and transform the services we can provide to our customer.

![this image shows a man and a woman talking to another woman in a house. One of the woman is holding a baby. ](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-Social-Loans-DMM?qlt=82&ts=1770797548255&dpr=off)

### £10bn funding ambition for social housing

We’ve announced a new £10 billion funding package for the UK’s social housing sector before the end of 2028, which when deployed will bring our total sector funding to over £35 billion since 2018.

![NatWest colleague delivering Financial Foundations workshop to customers and community members](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-Gurpreet-Kaur-Sohal-Manager-Walsall-Branch?qlt=82&ts=1770797528666&dpr=off)

### Reaching 50,000 people with financial education

We’re expanding our Financial Foundations programme to reach 1,000 people a week, helping more people build financial confidence.

## Our support across the UK

Find out more on how we’re supporting businesses across the nations and regions of the UK.

 

![Graffiti mural reads ‘WE ARE A REFLECTION OF OUR COMMUNITY’ in bold orange, green, and purple letters on a colourful wall, linked to Cardiff-based non-profit social enterprise Media Academy](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1051922562/productlistingmultip/product-listing-multiple-product/product_0/productimage.coreimg.png/1764682187086/nwg-media-academy-496x330.png)

We’re helping Media Academy Cymru (MAC), a Cardiff-based non-profit social enterprise, to expand operations and enhance its facilities for young people.

![Meeting room with people at Vanquish Fitness around a wooden table, laptops and notebooks, and a screen showing black t-shirt designs](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1051922562/productlistingmultip/product-listing-multiple-product/product_1/productimage.coreimg.png/1762863543028/nwg-vanquish-fitness-496x330.png)

We’re supporting Tottenham based athleisure and gym wear retailer Vanquish Fitness to fuel its growth ambitions.

![Quiet village in Cumbria with white cottages, a pub sign, green lawns, and hills in the background under cloudy skies.](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1051922562/productlistingmultip/product-listing-multiple-product/product_2/productimage.coreimg.png/1762863567628/nwg-strand-and-screes-496x330.png)

We’re backing Cumbrian business Strands & Screes Holdings Limited to create local jobs.

![Three branded cars parked outside CARS Accident Repair Centre, a grey building with a green and white sign.](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_1051922562/productlistingmultip/product-listing-multiple-product/product_3/productimage.coreimg.png/1762863587082/nwg-car-of-grimsby-496x330.png)

We’re helping Yorkshire-based vehicle repair business Cars of Grimsby to expand its business with a new, larger premises.

### Want to learn more?

Hear more from our customers on the support we provide across three leading businesses.

![This image shows the NatWest Group logo on a white background.](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_985479040__64411489/singlearticlev2/article_image.coreimg.png/1752572925527/nwg-gogarburn.png)

## Shareholder Hub

Get the latest information on our Annual Results and shareholder events.

 

![This image shows a NatWest Group colleague sat at a desk with a customer. They are looking at a laptop. ](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-Who-we-are-Hero-DMM?qlt=82&ts=1752579940167&dpr=off)

### Who we are

By turning our customers’ possibilities into progress we can succeed together.

![This image shows a picture of a NatWest branch](https://natwestgroup.com/_jcr_content/root/responsivegrid/container_985479040/productlistingmultip/product-listing-multiple-product/product_1/productimage.coreimg.png/1752579967583/nwg-our-brands-dmm.png)

### Our brands

Discover more about the brands serving over 20 million customers.

![this image shows a male and a female sat down looking to camera. ](https://nw.scene7.com/is/image/natwest/NWG-Office-1-DMM?qlt=82&ts=1752579992029&dpr=off)

### Work with us

Learn more about a career with us.

company.name: NatWest Group
company.about:
We’re the bank that turns possibilities into progress.

We understand our customers, their worlds, and what matters to them. We help them see the opportunities and navigate a rapidly changing world. And we’re simplifying our business so that we can anticipate our customers’ needs and react to them quickly.

Our business is UK centred and covers retail, commercial and private banking customers, with more offshore and further afield. Our services bring together our people’s incredible specialist knowledge with a sense of innovation, and carefully designed technology-driven journeys.

As one of the leading supporters of UK business, we’re using enterprise as a force for change. We’re focusing on the people and communities who have traditionally faced the highest barriers to entry and figuring out ways to remove them. 

That includes learning and education, whether directed at young people or building financial confidence in adults. And by fostering a leading learning culture in our business, our people also prosper.

evaluations.reasoning: NatWest Group has publicly documented production use of AI/ML inference for fraud detection. A 2025 engineering blog post on Camunda’s site describes how NatWest operationalized AI via Camunda to improve fraud detection, customer experience, and process automation, indicating a production deployment. A 2023 press release from Featurespace reports that NatWest’s fraud‑scam detection rate improved by 135% using Featurespace’s machine‑learning technology, again evidence of production AI inference. Additionally, NatWest’s own website includes an AI‑enabled scams guide that discusses how the bank applies AI to detect fraud. These sources collectively satisfy the criterion of publicly documented production AI/ML inference.

Camunda’s coverage describes a Fraud Center of Excellence with around 800 agents using 14 applications per case and a broad toolset, underscoring a move toward orchestrated AI within a headless, enterprise-scale process platform. enrichments.reasoning: The enrichment draws on NatWest's Privacy and Customer Confidentiality policy page, which explicitly mentions UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018, privacy-by-design principles, and P&CC training, and cites cybersecurity governance aligned with ISF SOGP/NCSC and PCI DSS for payment data to constrain AI deployments. The Paytia partnership page provides supporting PCI DSS context for card data handling if needed, but the primary authoritative constraint is the privacy page.

references.title: Protecting privacy and customer confidentiality | NatWest Group
references.snippet:
Protecting privacy is vital to retaining trust and growing customer engagement. We aim to address privacy requirements through the application of privacy by design and by default principles within our systems and processes. Everyone in NatWest Group must follow our Privacy and Client Confidentiality (P&CC) policy that sets out how we safeguard the personal data of our customers, colleagues and third parties, including our communities, suppliers and investors. Our policies and procedures also demonstrate our aim to comply with legal and regulatory requirements, including the UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018 and the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025.
[...]
All colleagues and contractors undertake mandatory P&CC training annually. This training is reviewed and updated every year to cover new topics and technologies, emerging risks and any lessons learned from the previous year. Job-specific training is also provided as necessary for colleagues, for example, privacy training to our data and artificial intelligence colleagues.
[...]
During 2025, there were a small number of breaches of GDPR and confidentiality (impacting a very small percentage of customers and employees) that we remediated, but there were no material reportable ‘personal data breaches’ under GDPR and no enforcement action by data protection authorities. We endeavour to respond to and remediate privacy complaints as quickly as we can.
[...]
To support our management of cybersecurity risk, policies are in place that set out the legal, regulatory and business requirements to protect Group information and services. They are reviewed at least annually against industry best practice and are available internally to all colleagues.
[...]
Policies are primarily reviewed against the Information Security Forum: Standard of Good Practice (ISF: SOGP) as well as other relevant publications by authorities such as the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC). They are also aligned to a number of international and industry standards including ISO 27001 and the National Institute of Standards and Technology Cyber Security Framework. Policy benchmarking is completed against the ISF: SOGP to confirm there are no control gaps. Throughout 2025, NatWest Group was certified by the IASME Consortium Ltd (IASME) in Cyber Essentials Plus, a recognised government-owned scheme operated by the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC).

references.title: NatWest and Paytia Partner to Deliver Secure Payment Soluti…
references.snippet:
Tyl by NatWest, a leading payment solutions provider, announced a strategic partnership with Paytia Limited to offer UK businesses secure card capture and payment services. This collaboration enables UK businesses to accept secure telephone payments over the phone, video, and chat channels while maintaining the highest security standards.
[...]
The partnership combines NatWest's banking heritage and merchant network with Paytia's PCI-compliant payment solutions. Businesses can now access secure phone, video, and chat payment solutions designed for simplicity, speed, and scalability, complementing Tyl's existing face-to-face card machine and online payment offerings.
[...]
The partnership delivers PCI-compliant phone payment capabilities that remove card data from telephone calls. Video and chat payment solutions are available for modern customer service teams, with smooth integration into existing payment infrastructure.
[...]
Businesses benefit from DTMF masking technology that prevents card data from entering call recordings, and agent-assisted payment solutions that keep card data out of business systems entirely.
[...]
Security is backed by NatWest's banking heritage. Full PCI DSS compliance across all payment channels, with dramatically reduced PCI compliance scope and cost. Payment data is completely isolated from business systems, reducing PCI compliance scope by up to 95%.
[...]
The solution uses channel separation technology to keep payment data separate from business operations, and includes automated payment processing for round-the-clock operations.
[...]
Curtis Nash, CEO of Paytia, said: "We're partnering with NatWest to bring our secure payment solutions to their merchant base. Our mission is to eliminate payment card data risk while creating smooth experiences for both businesses and their customers."
[...]
Businesses can access a complete suite of payment services backed by NatWest's banking heritage. Whether businesses need Tyl's face-to-face card machines, online payment gateways, or Paytia's secure phone, video, and chat payment solutions, they can access everything from one provider.
[...]
Paytia is a provider of secure payment solutions that help businesses take card payments safely over the phone, video, and chat channels. By removing sensitive payment card data from contact centres and business systems, Paytia enables organisations to reduce PCI compliance scope while delivering smooth customer experiences. For more information, visit www.paytia.com.

company.about:
We help industry-focused businesses modernize outdated systems and implement advanced technologies. We specialize in legacy software transformation and integration to reduce costs and improve operational efficiency.

At Evinent, our values are deeply rooted in our expertise in legacy system modernization, scalable IT solutions, and cost efficiency. We go beyond standard development to optimize infrastructure, enhance security, and drive measurable results for our clients.

evaluations.criterion: Enterprise company whose primary business is not AI infrastructure, AI inference, or AI-native products (exclude AI infrastructure vendors, AI cloud providers, GPU resellers, ML platform companies, foundation model labs, and AI-native startups; specifically NOT Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal, Groq, Cerebras, Crusoe, Coreweave, Nebius, Substrate AI, Inferact, Scale AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, Decagon)
evaluations.reasoning: Evinent GmbH is described as a software‑development firm that specializes in legacy‑software transformation and integration.  The internal company profile lists its industry as “Software Development” and its workforce as 14 employees, indicating a primary focus on custom software services rather than AI‑infrastructure or AI‑native products.  Web search results confirm that while Evinent offers AI‑development services and private AI solutions, it does not provide AI‑infrastructure, GPU resellers, or foundation‑model labs – it simply builds AI features for existing applications.  Because the company’s core offering is software development, not an AI‑infrastructure or AI‑native product platform, it meets the criterion of an enterprise company whose primary business is not AI‑infrastructure, AI‑inference, or AI‑native products.

properties.description: SymphonyAI is a private enterprise software development company with 1,275 employees and $462.5M annual revenue, founded in 2017 and headquartered in Palo Alto, CA. It builds vertical AI applications for industries like retail and manufacturing, delivering AI-based predictive and generative solutions; notably integrating AI inference into enterprise workflows via its IRIS Foundry platform with Microsoft Teams and Copilot. The company operates in data-sensitive sectors and has publicly documented use of production AI inference and SOC 2 compliance, demonstrating its focus on secure, industry-specific AI deployment.

## Purpose-built AI. Industry-proven results.

#### AI applications with domain IQ

### Retail

#### Built for retail’s full demand chain. Powered by real AI.

From seasonal surges to everyday staples, SymphonyAI helps high-velocity retailers optimize pricing, availability, and shelf execution in real time. Our vertical AI reads shopper signals, forecasts demand, and fine-tunes inventory and promotions—boosting sales, loyalty, and experience, store by store.

3.5% Saleslift Saleslift

98% Forecast accuracy Forecast accuracy

20% Increase in customer loyalty Increase in customer loyalty

### Financial Services

#### AI that’s built for scrutiny.

SymphonyAI is made for the messy reality of modern compliance where the cost of missed risk isn’t just fines, it’s trust and reputation. Our AI flags real risk, not noise. It slashes alert volumes while capturing new hidden risk and shows your team exactly why each decision was made, so you’re ready when regulators ask.

No black boxes. No hiding behind complexity. Just transparency, control, and speed at the scale your institution demands.

85% Fewer false positives Fewer false positives

70% Faster investigations Faster investigations

<50ms Fraud detection and scoring latency Fraud detection and scoring latency

### Industrial

#### When output drops, our AI knows why.

Every minute of downtime hits margin, flow, and delivery. SymphonyAI zeroes in on what’s going wrong—across assets, lines, or sites—and guides your team to act fast. No guesswork. No delays. Just real-time root cause analysis, smarter decisions, and continuous optimization from control room to connected worker.

Built for speed. Proven at scale. And always learning.

50% Reduction in downtime Reduction in downtime

4% Increase in throughput Increase in throughput

200% Decrease in training time Decrease in training time

From drafting SARs to rerouting inventory, they get work done with precision, context, and control.

Explainable. Auditable. Built for your business.

# The AI Microsoft trusts to get real work done

Named Microsoft’s AI Partner of the Year, SymphonyAI delivers production-ready, domain-specific AI on Azure.

From retail to manufacturing, our agentic solutions are already in market—diagnosing, deciding, and acting where the work gets done.

With SymphonyAI and Microsoft, it’s not a prototype. It’s AI in production.

company.name: SymphonyAI
company.about: SymphonyAI builds Vertical AI applications that help enterprises tackle their most complex, high-value challenges—like stopping financial crime, improving store performance, and boosting manufacturing efficiency. Trusted by more than 2,000 enterprise customers around the globe including 200 of the top financial institutions, top 25 CPGs, and many of the world’s largest grocers and industrial manufacturers, SymphonyAI delivers domain-trained applications and pre-built agents, ready to work on day one.

references.title: SymphonyAI Brings Advanced Industrial AI to Microsoft Teams and Microsoft 365 Copilot - SymphonyAI
references.snippet:
Palo Alto, Calif. –Sept. 16, 2025 – SymphonyAI today announced IRIS Foundry for Microsoft Teams, integrating industrial AI directly into Microsoft Teams and Microsoft 365 Copilot via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Optimized for the complex demands of manufacturing and energy companies, the integration delivers real-time operational visibility, automated workflows, and AI-driven insights—directly inside the tools plant operators, industrial workers, and frontline teams use every day.
[...]
- Get a demo
- Ask plain-language questions in Microsoft 365 Copilot such as, “Show me recent heat exchanger anomalies at Plant 7,” and instantly receive visual summaries within Teams.
[...]
- Trigger automated workflows to initiate maintenance, adjust parameters, or escalate alerts—without leaving Microsoft Teams.
[...]
- Customize domain-specific AI assistants using Microsoft Copilot Studio so companies can match plant-specific KPIs, workflows, and compliance needs.
[...]
- Knowledge Graph and Cortex AI: Leverage semantic relationships between equipment, events, people, and documentation to power Cortex—
[...]
Foundry’s AI engine for intelligent reasoning, search
[...]
Predictive Analytics and Asset Intelligence
[...]
Continuously analyze equipment performance
[...]
surface anomalies with contextual alerts automatically routed to relevant
[...]
- Process and Operations Intelligence: Track KPIs, identify performance trends, and enable predictive insights across production lines and plants through conversational interactions in Teams and Copilot.
[...]
- Accelerated Decisions: IRIS Foundry plant insights are surfaced directly in Microsoft Teams and Copilot leveraging MCP and AI Agents configured in Microsoft Copilot Studio, so frontline and operations teams can act faster — no analysts or extra tools required.
[...]
- Faster Time to Value: Because IRIS Foundry is integrated with Microsoft Teams and deployable via Copilot Studio, training is minimal, and adoption scales easily across enterprises and personas — maximizing existing Microsoft licensing investments.
[...]
- Scalable
[...]
“Manufacturing and energy companies need more than analytics—they need AI that acts instantly, where work happens,” said Prateek Kathpal, President, SymphonyAI Industrial. “By integrating IRIS Foundry with Microsoft Teams and Copilot, we’re putting real-time intelligence and automation in the hands of frontline workers, creating resilience and efficiency across operations.”
[...]
“By integrating IRIS Foundry with Microsoft Teams and Copilot, we’re transforming daily collaboration into a launchpad for real-time, AI-driven action,” said Kumar Abhimanyu, Senior Vice President & Global Head of Strategic Partnerships, SymphonyAI. “This deepens our integration with Microsoft in a meaningful way—combining their trusted platforms with our industrial AI to help customers accelerate decisions, boost efficiency, and realize tangible business impact.”
[...]
“Integrating IRIS Foundry with Microsoft Teams and Copilot puts real-time insights and AI-driven workflows right where frontline teams work, helping them solve problems faster and keep operations running smoothly,” said Dayan Rodriguez, Corporate Vice President, Manufacturing and Mobility, Microsoft.

references.title: SOC2 - SymphonyAI
references.snippet:
Developed by the American Institute of CPAs (AICPA), SOC 2 defines criteria for managing customer data based on five “trust service principles”—security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality and privacy.
[...]
Unlike PCI DSS, which has very rigid requirements, SOC 2 reports are unique to each organization. In line with specific business practices, each designs its own controls to comply with one or more of the trust principles.
[...]
These internal reports provide you (along with regulators, business partners, suppliers, etc.) with important information about how your service provider manages data.
[...]
SOC 2 certification is issued by outside auditors. They assess the extent to which a vendor complies with one or more of the five trust principles based on the systems and processes in place.
[...]
The security principle refers to protection of system resources against unauthorized access. Access controls help prevent potential system abuse, theft or unauthorized removal of data, misuse of software, and improper alteration or disclosure of information.
[...]
firewalls (WAFs
[...]
The availability principle refers to the accessibility of the system, products or services as stipulated by a contract or service level agreement (SLA). As such, the minimum acceptable performance level for system availability is set by both parties.
[...]
This principle does not address system functionality and usability, but does involve security-related criteria that may affect availability. Monitoring network performance and availability, site failover and security incident handling are critical in this context.
[...]
The processing integrity principle addresses whether or not a system achieves its purpose (i.e., delivers the right data at the right price at the right time). Accordingly, data processing must be complete, valid, accurate, timely and authorized.
[...]
However, processing integrity does not necessarily imply data integrity. If data contains errors prior to being input into the system, detecting them is not usually the responsibility of the processing entity. Monitoring of data processing, coupled with quality assurance procedures, can help ensure processing integrity.
[...]
Data is considered confidential if its access and disclosure is restricted to a specified set of persons or organizations. Examples may include data intended only for company personnel, as well as business plans, intellectual property, internal price lists and other types of sensitive financial information.
[...]
Encryption is an important control for protecting confidentiality during transmission. Network and application firewalls, together with rigorous access controls, can be used to safeguard information being processed or stored on computer systems.
[...]
The privacy principle addresses the system’s collection, use, retention, disclosure and disposal of personal information in conformity with an organization’s privacy notice, as well as with criteria set forth in the AICPA’s generally accepted privacy principles (GAPP).
[...]
Personal identifiable information (PII) refers to details that can distinguish an individual (e.g., name, address, Social Security number). Some personal data related to health, race, sexuality and religion is also considered sensitive and generally requires an extra level of protection. Controls must be put in place to protect all PII from unauthorized access.
[...]
While SOC 2 compliance isn’t a requirement for SaaS and cloud computing vendors, its role in securing your data cannot be overstated.
[...]
Enterprise IT division (Summit) undergoes regular audits to ensure the requirements of each of the five trust principles are met and that we remain SOC 2-compliant. Compliance extends to all services we provide, including web application security, DDoS protection, content delivery through our CDN, load balancing and Attack Analytics.

![TSB Visa debit card and footballer](https://tsb.co.uk/_jcr_content/root/container/container/container_1735748098/container/hero_copy_copy_copy.coreimg.png/1772529626592/tsb-visa-world-cup-26-footballer-hero.png)

![VISA FIFA World Cup 26™ logo](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/logos/visa-world-cup-26-logo.png "VISA FIFA World Cup 26™ logo")

# Win FIFA World Cup 26™ tickets, thanks to Visa

Make 5 purchases with your TSB Visa debit card before 31 March for the chance to win 1 of 5 pairs of tickets to the FIFA World Cup 26™ quarter finals in Miami.

[How do I enter?](https://tsb.co.uk/visa-promotion.html#prizes)

Opt in and make 5 eligible payments with the same TSB Visa debit card from 2 March - 31 March 2026 for your chance to win. [T&Cs apply](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/documents/current-accounts/prize-draws/FIFA_26_Prize_Draw_TCs.pdf). 18+ and UK resident.

Make more of your tax-free ISA savings

Not used all of your tax-free allowance? Now’s the time to give your savings a boost, before the tax year ends on 5 April.

T&Cs apply*

[Save today](https://tsb.co.uk/savings/isas.html)

Money worries If you’re struggling with the cost of living, you’re not alone. We’re here to help you get the support you need. [Visit our money worries page](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support/money-worries.html).

Bank of England: On 19 March 2026, the Bank of England announced no change to its base rate. Base rate remained at 3.75%. [Find out more](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support/bank-of-england-base-rates-recent-changes.html).

---

### Building your money confidence

Our experts answer your big questions to help you become more confident with your money every day.

[See all articles](https://tsb.co.uk/money-confidence.html)

![](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/money-confidence/MC-small-fixed-vs-tracker-mortgage.png)

[Borrow11th March 2026Fixed vs Tracker MortgagesBuying a home is a big step, and choosing the right mortgage is an important part of the process. The interest rate you pick can impact your monthly payments, your financial peace of mind, and how much you end up paying overtime.](https://tsb.co.uk/money-confidence/fixed-vs-tracker-mortgages.html)

![](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/money-confidence/MC-Small-Boost-Credit-Score.png)

[Save22nd January 2026How to boost your credit scoreYour credit score plays a big role in your life - from being approved for a mortgage or loan to getting better interest rates. Whether you’re starting from scratch or want to improve an already decent score, our guide can help.](https://tsb.co.uk/money-confidence/how-to-boost-your-credit-score.html)

![](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/business/icons/support-icon.png)

Need extra support?

There may be all sorts of reasons you might need us to work with you differently. We're here to help.

[Let us know](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support/support-for-individual-banking-needs.html)

![](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/business/icons/help-and-support-icon.png)

Got a question?

Trouble logging in or need help with your banking? Head to Help and Support for answers.

[Get help](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support.html)

![](https://tsb.co.uk/content/dam/tsb-public/images/business/icons/chat-icon.png)

Want to chat?

Give us a call, or chat to us online and in the app using TSB Smart Agent.

[Contact us](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support/contact-us.html)

### Independent service quality survey results

Published February 2026

As part of a regulatory requirement, an independent survey was conducted to ask approximately 1,000 customers of each of the 17 largest personal current account providers if they would recommend their provider to friends and family. The results represent the view of customers who took part in the survey.

[Find out more](https://tsb.co.uk/help-and-support/personal-service-quality.html)

 Overall service quality \r\n We asked customers how likely they would be to recommend their personal current account provider to friends and family. \r\n Ranking \r\n"}}" id="text-633b59559e">

### Overall service quality

We asked customers how likely they would be to recommend their personal current account provider to friends and family.

Ranking

![PCA Overall Service Quality. 1 Monzo 82%. 2 Starling 77%. 3 Nationwide 75%. 4 First Direct 74%. 5 Chase 72% 14 TSB 53%.](https://tsb.co.uk/_jcr_content/root/container/container/container_197265617/container_copy/columncontainer_2084/containertwo/image.coreimg.jpeg/1770975040958/tsb13472-pca-1-overall-service-quality-final.jpeg)

Authorised push payment (APP) scams rankings in 2024 \r\n \r\n Authorised push payment (APP) scams happen when someone is tricked into transferring money to a fraudster’s bank account. Information about TSB’s performance prior to the introduction of the reimbursement requirement in October 2024 can be found in PSR’s latest APP Scams Performance Report published in February 2026. \r\n"}}" id="text-b80563689f">

### Authorised push payment (APP) scams rankings in 2024

Authorised push payment (APP) scams happen when someone is tricked into transferring money to a fraudster’s bank account. Information about TSB’s performance prior to the introduction of the reimbursement requirement in October 2024 can be found in [PSR’s latest APP Scams Performance Report](https://www.psr.org.uk/media/b50p3mlm/psr-2024-app-scam-performance-data-february-2026.pdf) published in February 2026.

![FSCS Protected logo](https://tsb.co.uk/_jcr_content/root/container/container/container_197265617/container_827683521/additionalinfolist_c/firstaddinfo.coreimg.svg/1770300805789/fscs-portrait-hp.svg)

Financial Services Compensation Scheme

We're covered by the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS). This means that any money you hold in a TSB account is protected up to a limit of £120,000 - so you won't lose out.

[Find out more](https://www.fscs.org.uk/?WT.ac=AS00723_PCMS_SE_ALL_TI_LA)

![Take five. To stop fraud logo](https://tsb.co.uk/_jcr_content/root/container/container/container_197265617/container_827683521/additionalinfolist_c/secondaddinfo.coreimg.png/1763558339645/to-stop.png)

Is someone asking you to move your money?

Never transfer money to, or share passwords with, someone you don’t trust. This includes people online you’ve never met, too-good-to-be-true investments, and companies asking you to transfer funds directly to them.

[Stay ahead of fraud](https://tsb.c

company.name: TSB Bank
company.about:
At TSB we’re pioneering a new kind of banking for Britain – one that’s simple, straightforward and cares about people.

We offer friendly, honest and convenient banking that’s designed to meet our customers’ needs, not just ours. It’s what makes us different – that and our commitment to the communities we serve. We want to make banking better for everybody. 

With over 6,000 employees across our nationwide network of branches and operating centres, we're dedicated to the 5 million customers across the UK who do their banking with us. 

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=YJzJCd_pwy8

We will never ask you for any personal data on LinkedIn. Please protect yourself by not posting any personal information such as account details or contact details.

TSB Bank Plc is authorised by the Prudential Regulation Authority and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority and the Prudential Regulation Authority under registration number 191240, except for lending where we are licensed by the Office of Fair Trading. We subscribe to the Lending Code; copies of the Code can be obtained from www.lendingstandardsboard.org.uk. We are members of the Financial Services Compensation Scheme and covered by the Financial Ombudsman Service. (Please note that due to the schemes’ eligibility criteria not all TSB Business customers will be covered by these schemes).

Registered office: Henry Duncan House, 120 George Street, Edinburgh, EH2 4LH. Registered in Scotland No. SC95237.

evaluations.criterion: Enterprise company whose primary business is not AI infrastructure, AI inference, or AI-native products (exclude AI infrastructure vendors, AI cloud providers, GPU resellers, ML platform companies, foundation model labs, and AI-native startups; specifically NOT Fireworks, Together, Baseten, Modal, Groq, Cerebras, Crusoe, Coreweave, Nebius, Substrate AI, Inferact, Scale AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Perplexity, Decagon)
evaluations.reasoning: TSB Bank is a banking company that offers financial services such as current accounts, savings, credit cards, loans, and mortgages. It is not primarily focused on AI infrastructure, AI inference, or AI-native products. Therefore, it satisfies the criterion.

evaluations.criterion: Has publicly documented use of production AI/ML inference (e.g., RAG-based search, fraud detection, claims adjudication, embeddings, document processing, summarization, recommendation systems, or agentic workflows) — evidenced by engineering blog posts, conference talks, earnings call mentions, AI/ML platform job postings, or third-party press
evaluations.reasoning: TSB Bank has publicly documented use of production AI/ML inference.  In March 2022, Infosys delivered an AWS Contact‑Centre Intelligence solution that applies AI to detect call intent, customer sentiment and root‑cause analysis – a live, production‑grade inference system, as described in a public tech‑talk and blog post.  More recently, in March 2026, TSB announced it is using Adobe’s AI‑powered Experience Platform to deliver personalized customer experiences, another publicly documented production AI inference use.  Both sources are third‑party press/blog posts that meet the criterion’s evidence requirements.

evaluations.criterion: Operates under regulatory, contractual, or industry-specific data-handling obligations that affect cloud and AI deployment choices (e.g., HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2, GDPR, FedRAMP, FFIEC, NYDFS, state-level health regulations, customer-mandated data residency, or proprietary/sensitive customer data sensitivity)
evaluations.reasoning: TSB Bank, a UK retail bank, is required to comply with the UK GDPR and the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). The bank’s own privacy page states it complies with GDPR, and its 2023 policy summary explicitly references PCI DSS requirements for protecting cardholder data. These regulatory obligations directly influence how the bank can deploy cloud services and AI solutions, as they must ensure data protection, encryption, and auditability in accordance with GDPR and PCI DSS. Therefore, TSB Bank satisfies the criterion of operating under regulatory data‑handling obligations that affect cloud and AI deployment.

result: TSB Bank publicly documents production AI inference usage via Infosys’ AI-powered Contact Centre Intelligence (CCI) delivered on AWS (https://www.infosys.com/services/data-analytics/insights/analytics-solutions.html), which surfaces call intent, customer sentiment, and root-cause analysis to improve customer experience and reduce handle times. Adobe Firefly is used to accelerate marketing creative production, with Firefly Custom Models enabling on-brand assets to be produced in minutes rather than weeks (https://business.adobe.com/blog/tsb-turned-photography-process-into-ai-magic-with-adobe-firefly). Public disclosures do not publish exact inference spend, throughput metrics, GPU/frameworks, or latency constraints; the available sources describe the integration at a high level rather than detailing infrastructure (AWS case study: https://pages.awscloud.com/TSB-Bank-uncovers-conversational-insights-with-AI-powered-Contact-Centre-Intelligence-and-Analytics-Solution_2022_0315-MCL_OD.html).
enrichments.reasoning: I located public documentation showing TSB Bank’s production AI usage: Infosys’ AI-powered Contact Centre Intelligence (CCI) on AWS for TSB Bank—surface call intent, sentiment, and root-cause analysis; and Adobe Firefly usage for production creative workflows with Firefly Custom Models. I cite inline sources: Infosys page, AWS case study, and Adobe Firefly blog. No public figures for annual inference spend or detailed workloads are disclosed, so I describe the evidence and note gaps. Inline URLs included in the narrative for traceability.

### Datenschutzeinstellungen
Diese Website nutzt Cookies, um dir eine bestmögliche Benutzererfahrung bieten zu können. Dazu findest du hier eine Übersicht über alle verwendeten Cookies.
Technisch notwendig
Technisch notwendige Cookies sind solche, ohne die die Website nicht richtig funktionieren kann. Dazu gehören Cookies für den Zugriff auf sichere Bereiche und die CSRF-Sicherheit. Bitte beachte, dass die Standard-Cookies von Craft keine persönlichen oder sensiblen Informationen sammeln. Die Standard-Cookies von Craft sammeln keine IP-Adressen. Die von ihnen gespeicherten Informationen werden nicht an Pixel & Tonic oder Dritte gesendet.

Statistiken
Unsere Website verwendet den Web-Analyse-Dienst „Matomo“. Matomo ist eine Open-Source-Lösung zur Web-Analyse und verwendet Cookies.
Eine Erhebung der Sitzungsdaten erfolgt nur nach deiner vorherigen Einwilligung. Wenn du uns mit deinem Nutzungsverhalten dabei behilflich sein möchtest, unsere Website zu verbessern, kannst du hier durch Setzen des Häkchens deine Zustimmung erteilen.
Dein Browser setzt dann ein Opt-in-Cookie, welches die Speicherung von Matomo-Nutzungsdaten erlaubt. Wenn du deine Cookies löschst, wird auch das Matomo-Opt-in-Cookie entfernt. Bei einem erneuten Besuch unserer Website kannst du deine Einwilligung zur Erhebung deiner Sitzungsdaten erneut erteilen.
Die erhobenen Daten werden zur Analyse des Internetservices verwendet. Die Atruvia AG möchte damit die Webseite weiter verbessern und noch mehr an die Bedürfnisse der Nutzerinnen und Nutzer anpassen.

company.name: Atruvia AG
company.about:
Atruvia AG ist der Digitalisierungspartner in der Genossenschaftlichen FinanzGruppe. Das Unternehmen vereint jahrzehntelanges Expertenwissen in Banking und Informationstechnologie. Die speziell auf Banken zugeschnittenen IT-Lösungen und Leistungen reichen vom Rechenzentrumsbetrieb über das Atruvia Bankverfahren bis hin zur App-Entwicklung. Dabei nutzt das Unternehmen zukunftsweisende Technologien wie Smart Data und schreibt Prozessoptimierung und Regulatorik groß. 
  
Das Unternehmen mit Verwaltungssitz in Karlsruhe und Münster sowie Niederlassungen in München, Frankfurt und Berlin beschäftigt in der Unternehmensgruppe gegenwärtig fast 8.400 Mitarbeiter*innen, die gemeinsam einen jährlichen Konzernumsatz von rund 1,77 Milliarden Euro erwirtschaften. Zum Kundenkreis von Atruvia zählen rund 820 Volksbanken und Raiffeisenbanken in Deutschland, die Unternehmen der genossenschaftlichen Finanzgruppe sowie zahlreiche Privatbanken und Unternehmen anderer Branchen, darunter auch der ADAC. Atruvia betreut rund 164.000 Bankarbeitsplätze und verwaltet knapp 86 Millionen Kundenkonten. Mit bundesweit gut 32.000 eigenen Selbstbedienungsgeräten stellt das Unternehmen flächendeckend eine reibungslose Bargeldversorgung sicher. In ihren Hochsicherheitsrechenzentren betreibt Atruvia das Bankverfahren agree21.

Special introductory variable rate as low as Prime minus 1.51% for 9 months from the date of account opening. Currently 5.99% APR.2, 3 After the 9 months, the rate will be the standard approved variable rate, currently ranging between 7.50% to 14.85% APR. Rates will fluctuate based on changes to the index rate.2, 3 Excellent credit is required to qualify for the lowest rates.

## What can we help you with?

Loading

LightStream unsecured loan Loans up to $100,000 with low rates and no fees.

Unlock what’s possible with an unsecured loan. Good-to-excellent credit required.

[Apply now](https://consumerlending.digitalcommerce.truist.com/product/LST?factID=40007&icid=MerchLightstrea0625TruistComHome241) [Learn more](https://truist.com/loans/personal-loans/unsecured-personal-loans?factID=40007&icid=MerchLightstrea0625TruistComHome240)

Earn solid interest on your savings - with anytime cash access. Open your new Truist One Money Market Account today.

[Open now](https://deposits.digitalcommerce.truist.com/product/167?icid=MerchMMAOpen0624TruistComHome166) [Learn more](https://truist.com/savings/truist-one-money-market?icid=MerchMMALearn0624TruistComHome167)

![](https://static.truist.com/content/dam/truist-bank/us/en/images/content-images/non-lob/mom-and-daughter-painting-eggs.jpg)

Truist One Checking Fresh season. Fresh financial possibilities.

Step into both with a Truist One Checking account - with no overdraft fees, plus a Balance Buffer for eligible clients.1

[Open now](https://deposits.digitalcommerce.truist.com/product/195?icid=MerchTruistOneO0424TruistComHome142) [Learn more](https://truist.com/checking/truist-one-banking?cid=MerchLMT1Check0924TruistComHome194)

Flexible funds for life's big plans. Choose fixed or variable rates.

Earn cash back, fuel your next getaway, or level up your rewards.

With Zelle®, you can send money to friends and family right from your phone.2

Deposit checks,4 move money, get email or text alerts,5 and view your recent transactions - all in one app, designed with security in mind.
