company.name: Munich Re
company.about:
Munich Re is one of the world’s leading providers of reinsurance, primary insurance and insurance-related risk solutions. The group consists of the reinsurance and ERGO business segments, as well as the capital investment company MEAG. We are globally active and operate in all lines of the insurance business. 

Since our founding in 1880, we’ve been known for our unrivaled risk-related expertise and sound financial position. We offer customers financial protection when faced with exceptional levels of damage – from the 1906 San Francisco earthquake to the 2017 Atlantic hurricane season. We possess outstanding innovative strength, which enables us to also provide coverage for extraordinary risks such as rocket launches, renewable energies, cyber attacks, or pandemics.

We’re also playing a key role in driving forward the digital transformation of the insurance industry, and in doing so have further expanded our ability to assess risks and the range of services that we offer. Our tailor-made solutions and close proximity to our customers make Munich Re one of the world’s most sought-after risk partners for businesses, institutions, and private individuals.

Working at Munich Re is more than a career. It’s an invitation to tackle some of the world’s most pressing challenges. Together with our team, consisting of specialists from more than 80+ fields of expertise, we turn risks into exciting opportunities that move humanity forward. https://www.munichre.com/en/career

Munich Re Careers: p0060018221@munichre.com
Munich Re Imprint: www.munichre.com/en/service/imprint/index.html

Insurance revenue from insurance contracts issued (insurance revenue) fell by 0.7% to €60,412m (60,830m). In the reinsurance field of business, which generated insurance revenue of €38,731m (40,034m), the 3.3% decline was mainly due to negative currency effects (primarily associated with the US dollar) and the deliberate discontinuation of business that no longer met our return requirements. In the ERGO field of business, insurance revenue rose by 4.3% to €21,681m (20,796m), with both segments contributing to this growth.

We will process your data on the basis of the provisions of the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the German Federal Data Protection Act (BDSG), the German Telecommunications Digital Services Data Protection Act (TDDDG) and all other laws applicable to the processing of personal data.
[...]
As a general principle, we process your personal data in Germany or in countries that are European Union (EU) or European Economic Area (EEA) member states. If we need to transfer personal data to service providers or subcontractors outside the EEA, we will do so only if the European Commission has confirmed that the country’s level of data protection is sufficient, or if data protection is otherwise sufficiently guaranteed (for example, through binding corporate rules, or the European Commission’s Standard Contractual Clauses). You can write to the above-mentioned address to obtain detailed information and to learn more about the level of data protection at our service providers in non-EEA countries.

If the contents of the email refer to a contractual relationship, Munich Re will save the email. The email address will be saved exclusively for corresponding with you, and will not be disclosed to third parties. The legal basis for processing data transmitted in the course of sending an email is Art. 6(1f) GDPR. Our legitimate interest lies in communicating with you. By Claudia Cohen

January 12, 2026 at 3:06 PM UTC

Save

Societe Generale SA is scrapping a self-developed artificial intelligence tool in favor of Microsoft Corp.’s Copilot solution, highlighting the challenges even large lenders face in building out their own offering in the cost-intensive technology.

The French lender, which rolled out the tool known as SoGPT as an internal assistant for its workforce more than a year ago, has been decommissioning it since the end of last year, according to people familiar with the matter. The decision came after SocGen realized that the gap to other tools was widening, with employees complaining that SoGPT wasn’t up to date, the people said.

references.title: Societe Generale shares its strategy for integrating generative AI into the financial sector - Société Générale
references.snippet:
Last month, Societe Generale sponsored the AI For Finance 2024 event in Paris, where it shared its vision and strategy for the integration of artificial generative intelligence in the financial sector. This annual event brought together leading European experts in AI and finance, highlighting strategic advances and use case demonstrations in the field.
[...]
Noemie Ellezam, the Societe Generale group’s Head of AI, highlighted the current use of AI to enhance the client experience, with 300 use cases in production, 25% of which are directly aimed at improving the client experience.
[...]
Concrete examples: the use of chatbots and callbots to respond to clients’ most common queries queries, as well as the integration of AI to improve the relevance of offers and protect clients from fraud. SOBOT, the chatbot of SG Bank, handles almost 6,000 interactions a day and ELLIOT, the callbot of Boursobank, handles over 70% of client interactions from start to finish.
[...]
[How is AI changing customer relations within the bank?] We already use AI for a couple of years actually, to enhance customer experience. So this can be directly in the interaction with the clients. For example, callbots or chatbots in the retail or in the capital market space. But GenAI is really bringing a new building block to what we can do to enhance customer experience with AI, notably in the way we are dealing with incoming customer queries, for instance.
[...]
[How can generative AI improve existing use cases?] We can not only enhance the existing use cases that we have already, like chatbot, by better understanding, for example, the customer intention when they are calling us, but also to really display to them personalized customer journeys when we can have a full knowledge of the context of the clients and provide during the call, after the call, or to our front officers, advanced information about the customer to better serve their needs.

references.title: Data & GDPR, General Data Protection Regulation - Societe Generale Private Banking
references.snippet:
Data & GDPR, General Data Protection Regulation - Societe Generale Private Banking
[...]
- Setting up data use conditions, while ensuring their security and protection, with a decentralised organisation that is close to the businesses and the spread of a data culture, in respect of regulations, notably General Data Protection Regulation. Ensuring the technological and human resources for our ambition. Societe Generale invested in a big data platform to put data at the centre of its information systems. To attract and internally promote the top data-processing talent and skills, the Group relies on a targeted recruitment approach with traditional schools, the Grande Ecole du Numérique and an internal training programme known as Big Data Academy.
[...]
## Focus on GDPR, General Data Protection Regulation
[...]
In response to the proliferation of uses on personal data in all economic sectors, the European legislator published a new regulation called General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
[...]
From May 25th, 2018, all the processing of personal data (collection, analysis, storage, transfers, etc.) in Europe or carried out on European residents will be governed by this new regulation which reinforces:
[...]
- The rights individuals such as the need to obtain their consent to carry out certain treatments or the right to be forgotten
- Corporate accountability for data processing and penalties for non-compliance.
[...]
### The GDPR at Societe Generale
[...]
Societe Generale is aware of the importance for its clients to be able to retain control of their personal data, and it reaffirms its commitment to processing these data responsibly. This document explains its policy in this area and the key principles it follows in terms of protecting and using its clients’ personal data. The policy and principles will be adapted for each of the businesses and regions in which the Group operates, in accordance with local regulations.
[...]
To be compliant with this regulation and strengthen the trust of our customers and employees, Societe Generale group is committed to 25 guiding principles that constitute the common framework for all Group entities and departments. They are divided into 4 major themes:
[...]
- Objectives & commitments
- Consent management
- Roles and responsibilities
- Standards & Security
[...]
Read the data
[...]
- Societe Generale Luxembourg Data Policy
- Societe Generale Private Banking Monaco Data Policy (available in French)
- Societe Generale Private Banking France Data policy
- Societe Generale Group Data Policy
[...]
At the heart of the new European regulation, the Data Protection Officer (DPO) is a genuine "driving force" behind data protection compliance. His or her main tasks are:
[...]
- to inform and advise the data controller or subcontractor and their employees;
- to monitor compliance with the regulation and national data protection laws;
- to advise the organisation on carrying out impact studies on data protection and to verify their implementation;

She supports and advises the Group's Business Units and Service Units in terms of personal data protection, notably clients and employees data. Marie-Laure Constans is the privileged interlocutor for the French data protection authority (CNIL) and is responsible for the leading and supervision of Data Protection Officers appointed across the Societe Generale Group.

# Unser kostenloses Girokonto

0 € Kontoführung bei 700 € monatlichem Geldeingang oder für alle unter 28 Jahren

Kostenlose Visa Debitkarte inklusive

Bis zu [10% Cashback](https://dkb.de/privatkunden/girokonto/cashback?wt_mc=pk.cb.hp.b.nk.2603) auf Visa Kartenzahlungen

## Kreditkarten-Aktion: Wir bezahlen dir den Kartenpreis für ein ganzes Jahr

Beantrage bis zum 30.03.2026 eine Kreditkarte für deine nächste Reise und erhalte ein Guthaben von 30 Euro mit dazu – damit geht der Kartenpreis im ersten Jahr auf uns!

Zahle weltweit kostenlos überall dort, wo Visa akzeptiert wird – ob in New York, Bali oder Rom.

Genieße volle finanzielle Flexibilität mit individuellem Kreditrahmen, Notfallkarte bei Verlust und die perfekte Urlaubslektüre dank digitalem Kiosk.

![Vorderseite und Rückseite der Visa Kreditkarte der DKB](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/1eRxkFc9tU5WNelD3pWx1h/8fba2794b93c2791645ccfb2066bcdf6/DKB-Visa-Kreditkarte-Aktion_1290x860.png?fm=png&fl=png8)

## Modernisierungskredit mit Aktionszins

Der Frühling kommt. Dein Modernisierungsprojekt kann starten.

Aktion: Nutze unseren Top-Zinssatz bis zum 12.04.2026.

Flexible Laufzeiten: Bis zu 240 Monate – für maximale Planungssicherheit.

Komplett online: Schnelle Kreditentscheidung und Auszahlung erhalten.

![Eine Frau und ein Mann sitzen auf einem Dach mit Solarpanelen](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/3EaiysIjGI2NSCb09SNgkV/c3a66f46e75a57fa18974b8d05f1a05a/PRKU-566_1290x860.jpg?fm=webp)

## Beliebte Produkte

![](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/7MwyqZaLTV19pgQcCL5HHj/4199df59174cecad5ba9c14f1ebfc0b4/PRKU-699_1290x860.jpg?fm=webp)

Finde deine passende Finanzierung

### Günstige Kredite

Egal ob schneller Sofortkredit oder flexible Baufinanzierung – wir haben die Lösung.

![Zwei Hände halten DKB Visa Debitkarte](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/6tJpaN6j6nHVD3vKooHqxU/dea742de9b75b2e30a5331306ec2033f/PRKU-611_1290x860_Gemeinschaftskonto.png?fm=png&fl=png8)

Das Konto für Zwei

### Kostenloses Gemeinschaftskonto

Das perfekte Haushaltskonto für Paare oder WGs inklusive 2 Visa Debitkarten.

![Paar sitzt auf Mauer mit Kaffeebecher und Handy](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/70bhh0EMhORmtyOjMoiK7P/8e9e75d721054851e7ee4a1b845c99ec/PRKU-616_1290x860.jpg?fm=webp)

Mit Wertpapieren sparen

### Einfach Depot eröffnen!

Träume leben, fürs Alter vorsorgen, die Familie absichern: Brauchst du mehr Gründe für den Vermögensaufbau?

![50 Euro für deine Empfehlung](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/1frliPSul43Knz1OGYulgh/9cd3d9235acbb53d9c35b97feb199f8c/240426_Freunde_werben_50_320x240__1_.svg)

50 Euro für deine Empfehlung

Immobilienbewertung leicht gemacht: direkt & kostenlos

![Ein Frau hält einen Blumenstrauß in der Hand](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/28YrGy7OcZSd408vI8tQh4/adbac2d7f36262dccdb06a8923c46014/PRKU-797_1290x860.jpg?fm=webp)

Was ist wichtig?

### Finanzen im April

Ostern, Nebenkosten, Steuern: Diese Finanznews bringt der April.

![Mann mit Visa Debitkarte bezahlt am Kartenlesegerät einer Verkäuferin](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/76rKOoFVK7Q8fYGg6zlxHF/c76f70186e41caa744b466e6e1c84882/SKY-017_1290x860.jpg?fm=webp)

Finde deine passende Karte

### Kartentypen

Wir zeigen dir, was Debit-, Kredit- und Girokarten unterscheidet.

![Ein Mann putzt einen Motorroller.](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/3OMrlbdR6RGhsVLBRsLg7P/5db637eeaa9ffe353544ee47c2583e22/PRKU-484_GettyImages-1091319322_Scooter_Motorroller_Look.jpg?fm=webp)

Welche Geldanlage passt zu mir?

### Festgeld oder Tagesgeld

Das solltest du wissen, um dein Geld sicher und optimal zu parken und für dich arbeiten zu lassen.

![Google Play Store](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/6ZpnRNHA81n4GsXlYVG0aP/1853d0f16e919c6e94a80a4473833b45/Google-Play-Badge.png)

![Illustration eines Smartphone mit Girokonto Umsätzen in der DKB-App](https://images.eu.ctfassets.net/itz38ewm719j/42v9syol3JogxerpKsEcfP/f0dabfb9ac0326a54584c4d85002cdb2/DKB-App.png?fm=png&fl=png8)

Egal, ob du kurz deinen Kontostand checken oder eine Überweisung tätigen willst: Mit unserer DKB-App erledigst du deine Bankgeschäfte sicher, einfach und bequem von überall.

company.name: DKB | Deutsche Kreditbank AG
company.about:
Viele Banken wissen gar nicht, wofür sie eigentlich stehen – wir schon!

Seit unserer Gründung 1990 als Deutsche Kreditbank stehen wir für unseren Namen. Kredite für Deutschland. Für alles, was ein Mensch zum Leben braucht. Unser Anspruch: #geldverbesserer zu sein.

So war riskantes Investment-Banking noch nie eine Option für uns. Und wird es auch nie sein. Schon heute tragen wir mit über 52 Mrd. EUR (= ca. 73% unserer Kredite an Geschäftskund*innen) signifikant zu den Sustainable Development Goals der UN bei. Mehr als 5,2 Mio. Kund*innen vertrauen auf unsere Leistungen und empfehlen uns gern weiter.

Denn unsere über 5.000 Mitarbeitenden reden nicht nur über Herausforderungen wie bezahlbarer Wohnraum, saubere Energie, Bildungsvielfalt, lebenswerte Kommunen und flächendeckende medizinische Versorgung und Pflege, sondern packen die Dinge an und machen sie besser. Im Ergebnis sind wir eine der nachhaltigsten unter den Top-Banken in Deutschland.

Um sicherzustellen, dass wir die Bedürfnisse unserer Geschäftskund*innen verstehen, arbeiten in unseren Teams Branchen- und Bankfachleute zusammen. Mit lokaler Expertise entwickeln wir spezifische Lösungen und betreuen sie an ihrem Standort bei Zahlungsverkehr, Geldanlage und Finanzierungsfragen. Unser Versprechen: Wir bieten ausschließlich Leistungen und Services, an die wir auch selbst glauben.

DKB. Das kann Bank.

references.title: Innovationskooperation von DKB und OpenAI - DKB AG
references.snippet:
# Künstliche Intelligenz im Banking: OpenAI verkündet am deutschen Finanzmarkt eine direkte Innovationskooperation mit der Deutschen Kreditbank AG (DKB)
[...]
Die DKB stärkt mit der Zusammenarbeit ihren Anspruch, eine der First Mover für KI-Technologien am deutschen Bankenmarkt zu sein.
[...]
Gemeinsam mit OpenAI wird die digitale Transformation der Direktbank beschleunigt. Für Kund*innen ist die Einführung eines neuen KI-Sprach-Support im Banking geplant.
[...]
San Francisco / Berlin, 06. Mai 2025 – OpenAI und die Deutsche Kreditbank AG (DKB) geben heute eine direkte Innovationskooperation bekannt, um das digitale Banking voranzutreiben und ein schnelleres, personalisierteres und smarteres Kundenerlebnis zu bieten.
[...]
Die DKB ist bereits heute mit ihrem neuen Digital Agent eine der First Mover in der Adaption generativer KI im Kundenservice am deutschen Bankenmarkt (Digital Agent) und bei der Beschleunigung von dokumenten-basierten Kundenprozessen (DocAI).
[...]
Die Direktbank hat gemeinsam mit OpenAI den Anspruch, diese nachhaltige Vorreiterrolle beim Einsatz von generativer KI in Produkten, Services und Operations bis hin zur Erschließung neuer Geschäftsmodelle am deutschen Bankenmarkt auszubauen. Ein konkretes Projekt mit OpenAI ist die Einführung einer KI-Sprachsteuerung im Banking, die den 5,8 Millionen Kund*innen einen smarten Zugang zu Banking-Services ermöglichen soll. Zudem stellt OpenAI die Technologie für die Implementierung von Agentic AI Workflows zur Verfügung, um komplexe Dokumentenverarbeitung und damit verbundene Prozesse für ihre Mitarbeitenden zu vereinfachen.
[...]
Giancarlo Lionetti, Chief Commercial Officer at OpenAI said: “DKB is taking a bold step in reimagining the customer experience with AI. By integrating OpenAI’s models into their core operations, DKB is showing how AI can deliver tangible impact for employees and customers — improving speed, responsiveness, and service.”
[...]
Dr. Sven Deglow, CEO der DKB: „Die Zusammenarbeit mit OpenAI ist ein digitaler Booster für die DKB, um künstliche Intelligenz in einer bisher nicht realisierbaren Tiefe und Breite in unseren Prozessen zu verankern und damit die Kundenprodukte persönlicher und intelligenter zu machen. OpenAI steht global insbesondere mit seinen GPT-Modellen für bahnbrechende KI-Technologie und hat innerhalb weniger Jahre den globalen Zugang und die Nutzung von KI entscheidend geprägt. Wir sind stolz, das führende KI-Unternehmen an unserer Seite zu haben und freuen uns, schon bald unseren Kund*innen neue KI-Lösungen im Banking anbieten zu können. Generative KI ist für uns ein zentraler Hebel, um Wachstum zu beschleunigen und unsere Position als innovativer Vorreiter gegenüber dem Wettbewerb auszubauen.“
[...]
Sascha Dewald, SVP Corporate Development & Communications, DKB: „Mit dem Digital Agent hat die DKB bereits den ersten Schritt für die direkte Interaktion zwischen generativer KI und Bankkund*innen geschaffen – und das mit großem Erfolg. Die Kooperation mit OpenAI ist ein weiterer prägender Schritt für unsere digitale Transformation und ermöglicht uns die Produktentwicklung auf Basis gemeinsamer KI-Projekte zu beschleunigen und Zugang zu neuesten Technologien von OpenAI zu erhalten. KI ist ein wesentlicher Bestandteil der Unternehmensstrategie der DKB und für uns ein Core-Asset im Wettbewerb um heutige und künftige Kund*innen.“

company.about:
Bank of America is one of the world's largest financial institutions, serving individuals, small- and middle-market businesses and large corporations with a full range of banking, investing, asset management and other financial and risk management products and services. The company serves approximately 56 million U.S. consumer and small business relationships. It is among the world's leading wealth management companies and is a global leader in corporate and investment banking and trading.

This LinkedIn company page is moderated. For more information, please visit: https://bit.ly/32FDdQr.

For account issues, please visit: https://bit.ly/2GeTIeP.

references.title: Using Machine Learning and AI to Transform Cyber Security
references.snippet:
Using Machine Learning and AI to Transform Cyber Security
[...]
Machine learning is changing cyber-fraud — and helping fight it
[...]
In an increasingly connected digital economy, rapid advances in technology such as machine learning (ML) are being leveraged for both business objectives and malicious intent. As more cyber criminals harness the power of the latest ML tools to launch ever-more effective scams and thefts, businesses are also adopting the technology to sharpen their cyber-defenses. In this environment, it’s critical not only to safeguard your business against ML-enabled cyber-fraud, but also to understand the risks involved in deploying the technology in your fraud-detection arsenal.
[...]
As criminals continue using cutting-edge technologies to enable fraudulent activities, tech-adept businesses are countering these advances by adopting ML and other AI tools to improve their cyber-defenses. A recent survey by Forbes found that more than half of U.S. businesses use cyber security and fraud management tools that employ some form of AI.1 These tools help them identify indicators of compromise across multiple data access points, monitor activity and rapidly respond to cyber threats, dramatically reducing labor hours required for initial triage of potential threats.

[...]
At the same time, cyber-criminals are adopting new ML technologies to research and design targeted and sophisticated fraud schemes. They’re using ML to automate and expedite more convincing and personalized phishing attacks, including investment scams and business email compromise. They are also using generative AI tools (e.g., search engines, chat clients and learning systems), voice-cloning software and deepfake videos to create incredibly detailed and realistic lures. (For more on deepfake technology and cyber crime, see “How to protect your business from deepfakes.”)
[...]
Forbes asked U.S. business leaders how they currently use or plan to use AI. Top responses: improved customer experience (56%) and cyber security and fraud management (51%).1
[...]
Chat clients and learning systems can fast-track cyber-scammers’ social engineering methods and improve their effectiveness by generating customized bait messages based on vast amounts of personal and social data, making them much more personalized and persuasive. Natural language technologies help create complex and multifaceted phishing messages that are scrubbed of obvious grammatical errors, misspellings and oddly worded sentences. Cyber criminals are also using AI and ML tools to attack and gain unauthorized access to accounts and networks, often by analyzing behavior patterns or using ML to identify vulnerabilities.
[...]
Ultimately, people — and not technology alone — form the foundation of effective cyber security. Employee cyber security awareness and training for new ML solutions present an opportunity to refocus your organization on creating a culture of security, one in which employees have the knowledge and agency to proactively spot and then protect against cyber-crimes.
[...]
For additional information on machine learning and cyber-fraud, see Bank of America resources on fraud and cybersecurity.

references.title: Agentic AI & Autonomous AI Agents - Latest Trends in AI
references.snippet:
Agentic AI & Autonomous AI Agents - Latest Trends in AI

# The new wave: Agentic AI

Agentic AI represents a generation of increasingly powerful foundation models that may spark a corporate efficiency revolution.

January 2025

## Key takeaways

- New and disruptive AI capabilities are emerging rapidly – so rapidly, in fact, that the next wave, Agentic AI, is arriving before chatbots and copilots reach version 2.0.
- Agentic AI represents a new generation of increasingly powerful foundation models that act as operating systems for autonomous, action-taking, digital agents capable of enhanced reasoning and decision-making, as well as increasingly disruptive chatbots and copilots.
- Over the next decade, these fully autonomous agent and robot fleets may ultimately alter verticals heavily reliant on human capital and spark a corporate efficiency revolution that transforms the global economy.

Read our full analysis for a more in-depth look at these trends.

See all insights

result: Bank of America’s Erica operates in production AI using NLP grounded in ML, not generative models, mapping user input to about 700 intents and triggering hundreds of defined workflows across customer and employee use cases. Public disclosures show Erica has achieved massive scale: since 2018 it has supported over 3.2 billion client interactions and, last year, nearly 700 million interactions from about 20.6 million users (≈58 million interactions per month). The bank cites a deliberately restrained inference stack (not using large language models) and relies on curated intents and traditional ML; for example, CashPro Chat accounts for 65% of client interactions with Erica handling >40% of interactions, and Erica for Employees is used by >90% of staff, reducing IT help-desk calls by around 50%. Estimated annualized AI inference spend is not publicly disclosed by BoA.
enrichments.reasoning: Sourced from BoA press releases and industry coverage: Erica's 3.2B+ total interactions since 2018 and 58M interactions/month (2025 release), 700M interactions last year from 20.6M users (2026 release); Erica architecture described as NLP/ML with ~700 intents and hundreds of workflows (American Banker), and BoA’s Erica page notes NLP grounded in ML and non-LLM approach; CashPro Chat accounts for 65% of client interactions and Erica handles >40% of interactions, with Erica for Employees used by >90% of staff, reducing help-desk calls by ~50%. Public inference spend is not disclosed.

references.title: PCI Compliance - Merchant Help
references.snippet:
The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS or PCI), is a minimum set of security requirements designed so that *all *companies who accept card payments protect cardholder data. The PCI Security Standards Council (formed by the card brands) sets the standards that are then enforced by the card brands, banks, acquirers, etc. By adhering to the security standards, businesses can protect themselves against security breaches, safeguard customer data, and protect the integrity of electronic payments.
[...]
It's important to protect the data of your business and your employees. Between malware and virus threats, remote-access attacks, and social engineering, it's important to take the proper precautions to keep your computers, networks, and servers secure. While the purpose of the PCI DSS is to protect card data from hackers and thieves, by following this minimum standard across your organization, you can keep all of your data secure, avoiding costly data breaches and protecting your employees and your customers.
[...]
Every merchant who accepts credit or debit cards as a method of payment is required to comply with the PCI DSS. Compliance depends on your merchant level. Merchant Levels are defined by the card brands and are based on your transaction volume (not the transaction amounts, just the number of card transactions).
[...]
Transaction Volume determines PCI Merchant level
[...]
Table of transaction volume and merchant levels|Transaction Volume|Merchant Level|
[...]
|More than 6 million card transactions per year - all channels|1|
[...]
|Between 1 and 6 million card transactions per year - all channels|2|
|Between 20,000 and 1 million e-commerce ONLY card transactions per year|3|
|Less than 20,000 e-commerce card transactions and less than 1 million overall transactions per year|4|
[...]
Bank of America's Merchant PCI L1 and L2 program assigns a PCI Analyst. The PCI Analyst is responsible for receiving the annual PCI Validation documentation, they will then review the submitted documentation for proper validation of PCI Compliance status.
[...]
Additionally, the PCI Analyst acts as a resource for the merchant throughout the year in regards to PCI clarifications and questions, updates, newly announced PCI DSS version changes, etc. This resource is provided at no additional cost. They also will communicate with merchants prior to their annual due dates and will coordinate routine communications to stay apprised of status.
[...]
Bank of America simplifies the process of self-assessment by providing you with all of the tools you need to achieve, maintain and validate your PCI compliance.
[...]
Log in to PCI Assist to complete compliance. After entering some simple information about your environment, you are presented with a series of questions that will do these things:
[...]
* Direct you to the proper Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ).
[...]
* Limit the number of questions you need to answer. **Example:** If you indicate that you do not have WiFi, we will pre-answer all of the WiFi related questions for you.
[...]
* Present the questions using non-technical language where possible and provide clear help text with examples and suggestions
[...]
* Allow you to stop at any point and return without losing any of your answers
[...]
* Provide you with help in the form of chat, email or call center support during normal business hours where agents can explain the requirements and help you navigate the portal
[...]
* Provide you with tools like vulnerability scanning (if required) and downloadable Security Policy templates, etc.
[...]
You will not be required to print SAQs or Network Scan Results and send those to the bank, as the portal records your compliance status for you.

## Principal Participating Organizations

## Bank of America

Bank of America is one of the world’s leading financial institutions, serving individual consumers, small and middle-market businesses and large corporations with a full range of banking, investing, asset management and other financial and risk management products and services. The company provides unmatched convenience in the United States, serving approximately 67 million consumer and small business clients with approximately 3,900 retail financial centers, approximately 16,000 ATM and award-winning digital banking with approximately 56 million verified digital users. Bank of America is a global leader in wealth management, corporate and investment banking and trading across a broad range of asset classes, serving corporations, governments, institutions and individuals around the world. Bank of America offers industry-leading support to approximately 3 million small business households through a suite of innovative, easy-to-use online products and services. The company serves clients through operations across the United States, its territories and approximately 35 countries. Bank of America Corporation stock (NYSE: BAC) is listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

![Rules Inventory](https://regpass.ai/assets/illustrations/Illustration-Rules-Inventory.svg)

### Rules Inventory

Understand your regulatory obligations with our rules inventory enriched with AI insights. Overlapping obligations are consolidated into canonical obligations for clear traceability.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/illustrations/Illustration-Controls-Policy%20Mapping.svg)

### Policy & Controls Mapping

AI suggests mappings from obligations to your policies and controls with clear rationale. Quickly identify gaps and overlaps and ask the AI to recommend enhancements.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/illustrations/Illustration-Compliance-Co-Pilot.svg)

### Regulatory Policy Advisor

Ask in plain English and get actionable guidance. The chatbot is your AI compliance co-pilot, parsing complex regulations into understandable insights.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/illustrations/Illustration-Controls-Policy%20Mapping.svg)

## Business Profiles: Focus On The Signal, Not The Noise.

Set up your business once — jurisdictions, licences, regulated activities, products, client types — and we'll filter the noise.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/funnel-white.svg)

### Entity-Aware Applicability

Focus by jurisdiction, legal entity, desk, product, and client type. We auto-triage alerts and rules against your Business Profile.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/blend-white.svg)

### Understand Why Each Rule Applies

Every item explains applicability with clear reasoning and links to sources—so reviewers see the evidence, not just the outcome.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/user-round-cog-white.svg)

### Tailored Views

Owners see only what matters to them—trading sees trading, ops sees ops. Less noise, faster decisions, cleaner audits.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/sliders-horizontal-white.svg)

### One-Time Setup

Define once, reuse everywhere: extraction, applicability, mappings, reporting—all driven by your Business Profile.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/illustrations/Illustration-Compliance-Co-Pilot.svg)

## The Compliance Co-Pilot to 10× Your Team's Impact.

AI that amplifies human expertise, reduces risk, and drives efficiency.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/list-todo-white.svg)

### Extract & Prioritise

Automatically identify relevant obligations from new rules and highlight what matters most for your business, routing them to the right owners.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/brain-white.svg)

### Full Coverage Insight

See at a glance which areas are covered and where gaps exist, ensuring nothing slips through across your business units and products.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/globe-lock-white.svg)

### Map with Confidence

Connect obligations to your policies and controls with clear rationale, so reviewers can approve quickly and confidently.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/book-check-white.svg)

### Close the Loop

Trace requirements all the way to evidence, making audits, board reporting, and compliance decisions faster and more reliable.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/file-search-2-white.svg)

### Work Smarter

With regulatory insights at their fingertips, users can make substantial savings in external counsel spend.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/sparkles-white.svg)

### Work Faster

Cut time spent on compliance tasks by up to 75% with our streamlined workflows and AI-enhanced automation.

## Why RegPass™

Purpose-built for regulatory change management, not a generic AI tool retrofitted for compliance.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/shield-user.svg)

### Built by Experts

[Braithwate](https://www.braithwate.com/) has codified decades of regulatory change expertise into RegPass™’s knowledge graph, giving your team instant access to deep domain knowledge.

---

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/user-lock.svg)

### Humans in Control

Every AI suggestion is explained, auditable, and ready for human approval—so your team stays in charge.

---

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/sparkles.svg)

### AI at its Heart

AI drives the workflow, turning alerts into actionable obligations, not an afterthought bolted on.

---

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/list-checks.svg)

### End-to-End Traceability

Track every change from regulatory alert to assurance, giving complete visibility across your compliance lifecycle

---

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/webhook.svg)

### Open & Extensible

Clean APIs, webhooks, and integrations let you extend RegPass™ without vendor lock-in.

---

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/file-clock.svg)

### Transparent by Default

All outputs link back to sources, rationale, and decisions—so you always know why it’s right for your business.

## What You'll See in a Demo

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/circle-check-big-brand-400.svg)

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/circle-check-big-brand-400.svg)

- Explore Traceability from Rules → Obligations → Controls/Policies
- Ask regulatory questions in plain English and see the exact sources behind the answer

## Built for Trust

Enterprise-grade security, compliance, and risk management.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/shield-user-white.svg)

### Model Risk: Managed

All AI suggestions require human approval. Confidence, rationale, and overrides are captured.

![](https://regpass.ai/assets/icons/lucide/globe-lock-white.svg)

SSO/OIDC, RBAC, encryption in transit/at rest, regional and on-prem deployment options. We prioritize security at every layer.

references.title: Introducing RegPass: A New Agentic Paradigm for Regulatory Change Management - A-Team
references.snippet:
RegPass, founded by former Baringa Partners compliance leader James Nicholls, sits squarely in this third wave. What began as a specialist advisory firm has evolved into an AI-native regulatory intelligence platform that blends knowledge graphs, large language models, and proprietary data structures. Its ambition is straightforward but far from simple: to make regulatory change management precise, contextual, and operationally relevant.
[...]
Yet the shift from consultancy to technology did not stop there. As the firm grew, it faced the same scaling challenges as many professional services businesses: the need for consistent regulatory inputs, reusable analysis structures, and more efficient internal research. That need coincided – fortuitously – with the advent of modern AI.
[...]
At the heart of RegPass today is a regulatory knowledge graph, a machine-readable representation of real-world financial entities, licences, products, trading processes, and business relationships. The platform ingests daily regulatory updates – “a firehose of content”, as Nicholls puts it – from providers such as Regalytics and applies an AI parser to perform entity recognition, linking each piece of information into the knowledge graph.
[...]
updates,” he explains. “At the
[...]
The graph model enables two things the previous generation of tools struggled with. First, it allows updates to be interpreted in the context of a specific business. Second, it gives large language models (LLMs) a reliable ground truth to consult when assessing applicability, ensuring that reasoning is anchored in domain structure rather than free-form inference.
[...]
Even with strong modelling, firms vary dramatically in their data maturity. Some can supply structured inventories of desks, licences, client types, and product taxonomies. Others have only spreadsheets and policy documents. RegPass uses LLMs to close this gap without forcing clients into rigid data standardisation programmes.
[...]
“We can adapt the level of data that we put in to what the client is able to provide,” Nicholls says. Richer inputs produce more nuanced outputs, but value is delivered across the maturity spectrum.
[...]
The knowledge graph provides the structure that keeps this fluency aligned with regulatory meaning, grounding the model’s reasoning in financial domain architecture.
[...]
Nicholls believes the next frontier is agentic compliance – systems that not only interpret regulation but perform tasks autonomously under human oversight.
[...]
“2026 is going to be the year that we see genuine agentic compliance,” Nicholls predicts. Early candidates include high-volume, low-consequence processes such as gifts and entertainment approvals or pre-clearance of employee trades. “This stuff really shouldn’t be that difficult,” he notes.
[...]
The shift raises governance questions – how to decompose processes, how to define oversight and how to detect errors in real time. But the direction of travel is clear: compliance is moving from reactive interpretation to proactive, automated execution.
[...]
RegPass exemplifies a broader transformation in regulatory technology. As regulatory data volumes explode and AI becomes embedded in enterprise infrastructure, firms are seeking systems that understand how their businesses operate, how processes interlock and how rules flow across activities.
[...]
As the new year unfolds, the market will test how far firms are willing to go in letting agents not just analyse, but act. What is clear is that the compliance architecture of the next decade will look vastly different from the spreadsheet-driven world that preceded it – and platforms like RegPass are helping define what comes next.

references.title: Privacy Policy
references.snippet:
Privacy Policy

# Privacy Policy

We respect your privacy and process personal data in accordance with GDPR and applicable laws.

## 1. Who we are (Controller)

Braithwate Ltd ("RegPass", "we", "us", "our") is the controller of personal data processed via this website and related services. If you have questions, contact privacy@regpass.ai.

## 2. Personal data we collect

### 2.1 Data you provide

- Communications sent to us (support, sales inquiries).
- Preferences (areas of interest, firm type) submitted via forms.
- Contact details (name, business email, company, role) via forms (e.g., "Book a demo").

### 2.2 Data collected automatically

- Cookies and similar technologies to improve site functionality and measure performance.
- Technical data (IP address, device/browser type, OS, approximate location).
- Usage data (pages visited, links clicked, time on page).

## 3. Purposes and legal bases

- Analytics and product improvement (legitimate interests) with privacy safeguards.
- Security, fraud prevention, and compliance (legitimate interests and legal obligations).
- Marketing communications with your consent; you can withdraw consent at any time.
- Provide services and respond to requests (legitimate interests or pre-contractual necessity).

## 4. Sharing and processors

We may share data with trusted service providers (processors) who support our operations (e.g., hosting, analytics, CRM, email). These parties process data under our instructions, subject to confidentiality and data protection agreements. We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, property, or safety.

## 5. International transfers

Where data is transferred outside the UK/EEA, we use appropriate safeguards such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs), UK IDTA/Addendum, and additional technical/organizational measures as needed.

## 6. Retention

We retain personal data only as long as necessary for the purposes described or as required by law. Criteria include the nature of data, the purpose of processing, and legal/operational requirements. We periodically review and securely delete data that is no longer needed.

## 7. Your rights

- Right to lodge a complaint with your data protection authority.
- Right to withdraw consent where processing is based on consent.
- Access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection to processing.

To exercise rights, contact privacy@regpass.ai. We may need to verify your identity before responding.

## 8. Cookies

We use necessary cookies for basic site functionality and may use analytics/performance cookies with your consent, where required. You can manage cookies via your browser settings. See our Cookie Notice for details (coming soon).

## 9. Security

We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect personal data against loss, misuse, and unauthorized access. No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure; we work continuously to improve our controls.

## 10. Children’s data

Our site and services are intended for business users. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.

## 11. Changes to this policy

We may update this policy from time to time. We will indicate the latest revision date below and, where appropriate, notify you of significant changes.

## 12. Contact

Questions or requests: privacy@regpass.ai

Last updated: October 13, 2025

references.title: RegPass™ – Secure by Design
references.snippet:
RegPass™ – Secure by Design

# Secure by Design

SSO/OIDC, RBAC, encryption in transit/at rest, and regional or on‑prem deployment options. Security prioritized at every layer.

## Identity & Access

- Session security with configurable timeouts and device/session management.
- Granular project/workspace access for teams, clients, and auditors.
- Role‑Based Access Control (RBAC) with least‑privilege roles and scoped permissions.
- Enterprise SSO via OpenID Connect (OIDC) with your IdP (e.g., Azure AD/Entra, Okta, Google Workspace).

We integrate with your identity strategy so access follows your policies. Centralized auth + fine‑grained authorization keeps data protected and auditable.

## Data Protection

- Options for private networking and IP allow‑listing to reduce exposure.
- Data minimization, access logging, and retention controls.
- Key management with separation of duties and rotation policies.
- TLS for data in transit; strong encryption at rest for databases, files, and backups.

Confidential data is protected end‑to‑end with modern cryptography and strict controls, paired with clear audit trails for every access.

## Deployment Options

- Configuration for egress restrictions and customer‑managed integrations.
- On‑premises options for sensitive environments.
- Private cloud/VPC deployments with private endpoints.
- Regional hosting and data residency options.

Choose the right deployment model for your risk posture and regulatory requirements—from regional SaaS to private cloud or on‑prem.

## Operational Security

- Vulnerability management and periodic third‑party testing.
- Secure SDLC with code review and dependency scanning.
- Secrets management, backup/restore, and disaster recovery testing.
- Centralized logging, monitoring, and alerting across services.

Operational controls are part of the product lifecycle—so security doesn’t stop at deployment. We continuously improve defenses and visibility.

# Transforming Enterprises - Data to AI to ROI

Inferenz helps enterprises transform data into intelligence and AI into real business results using scalable solutions, problem-first strategy, and ROI-led execution.

[Book a Strategy Consultation](https://inferenz.ai/contact-us/)

## Inferenz Approach

The iDAR™ Framework is Inferenz's signature approach that guides enterprises from data readiness to scalable AI and measurable business impact. It ensures every initiative moves with clarity, velocity, and ROI accountability.

![Initiate](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/elem-1.png)

#### Initiate

- Problem-first focus
- Enterprise data readiness
- AI roadmap

![Design](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/elem-2.png)

#### Design

- Scalable data foundation
- Aligned data & architecture
- Quick wins tailored to your business

![Accelerate](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/elem-3.png)

#### Accelerate

- Operationalize AI in workflows
- AIOps/MLOps reliability
- Responsible and Ethical AI

![Realize](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/elem-4.png)

#### Realize

- Measure impact, ROI
- Drive adoption
- Scale enterprise AI

## Services

End-to-End Data & AI Services Built for Scalable & Measurable Enterprise Impact.

### Strategy & Consulting

We define platform, data and AI strategies aligned to business goals. Our roadmaps guide strategy, architecture and execution toward measurable, scalable ROI.

### Data & Cloud Modernization

We modernize data landscapes with engineering, quality, governance and cloud expertise. We convert fragmented data into compliant, trusted, analytics-ready assets for smarter, faster decisions.

### AI & Automation

We build AI applications, agentic systems and automated workflows that reduce operational friction. Our solutions improve accuracy, efficiency and experience across business processes.

### Operationalize & Scale

We operationalize AI, data and cloud with AIOps, DevOps, LLMOps and CloudOps. Our frameworks ensure automation, reliability, observability and scalable performance in production.

## Industries

Enterprise Ready AI That Solves Real Problems and Drives ROI.

![Healthcare](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Healthcare.png)

## Healthcare

Elevates patient outcomes, supports caregivers, and improves care quality efficiently.

![ Insurance](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Insurance.png)

## Insurance

Modernizes processes, improves risk insight, and delivers seamless policyholder experiences.

![ Hi-Tech](https://inferenz.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Hi-tech-.png)

## Hi-Tech

Fuels innovation, scales intelligent systems, and maximizes digital performance.

company.name: Inferenz
company.about:
Inferenz is a next-generation, data and AI native solutions company headquartered in Ahmedabad, India, with a presence in Pune and Texas (USA). We are focused on enabling enterprises to transform data into measurable business outcomes. With deep expertise across Healthcare and HiTech industries, we partner with global enterprises from multi billion dollar organizations to mid-large companies, helping them navigate their journey from Data → AI → ROI.

Our solution led approach combines strategy, consultation, implementation, and scale. We specialize in building modern data and AI ecosystems that integrate diverse data sources including IoT devices, EHR/EMR systems, CRMs, and third-party platforms.

Service Offerings:

➡️ Data Foundation: Ingestion, pipelines, integration, and data warehouse modernization

➡️ Intelligent Insights: Advanced analytics, predictive and prescriptive models

➡️ AI Across the Gradient: From RPA and automation to Generative AI and Agentic AI solutions

➡️Scalable Execution: End-to-end deployment of AI solutions in production environments

Why Inferenz?

➡️ 96% client retention rate driven by long-term partnerships and customer satisfaction

➡️10% of capacity invested in R&D, continuously shaping next-gen AI capabilities

➡️ Part of NVIDIA Inception Program, Microsoft Startup Group, and NASSCOM AI Champions Cohort

➡️ Strong cloud-native partnerships with Snowflake, Databricks, AWS, and other hyperscalers

➡️ “Small enough to care, large enough to scale” - personalized attention with enterprise-grade execution

At Inferenz, we operate in consultative mode, defining the right problem statements, designing pragmatic solutions, and scaling them into production ensuring enterprises unlock true business value from their data and AI investments.

company.name: Groupe Crédit Agricole
company.about:
Crédit Agricole Group is the leading partner of the French economy and one of the largest banking groups in Europe. It is the leading retail bank in Europe as well as the first European asset manager, the first bancassurer in Europe and the third European player in project finance.

Built on its strong cooperative and mutual roots, its 157,000 employees and the 27,423 directors of its Local and Regional Banks, the Crédit Agricole group is a responsible and responsive bank serving 54 million customers and 12,1 million mutual shareholders.

Thanks to its universal customer-focused retail banking model – based on the cooperation between its retail banks and their r elated business lines –, the Crédit Agricole group supports its customers’ projects in France and around the world: day-to-day banking, home loans, consumer finance, savings, insurances, asset management, real estate, leasing and factoring, and corporate and investment banking.

Crédit Agricole also stands out for its dynamic, innovative corporate social responsibility policy, for the benefit of the economy. This policy is based on a pragmatic approach which permeates across the Group and engages each employee.

- Continous Cyber RIsk Quantification(CRQ)
- ROI/RRI-based prioritization
- Automated Executive Reporting

- Board-ready financial impact in real time
- ROI/RRI-based prioritization and budget guidance
- Automated reporting for CISO, CRO, CFO

# The Financial Intelligence Platform for Cyber – Continuous. Defensible. Real.

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/CRQ1.png)

### Dynamic Cyber Risk Quantification & ROI

Always-On CRQ that quantifies risk in dollars and proves ROI

- Model financial impact across evolving business and threat scenarios
- Build and run custom what-if simulations in minutes
- Set risk tolerance levels and track exposure against thresholds

[Learn More](https://quantara.ai/cyber-risk-quantification/)

### Cyber Threat & Exposure Intelligence

Business-aligned visibility into the top 1% of risks

- Map exposures by business unit, region, and critical asset
- Prioritize by financial impact, not volume or severity scores
- Link every exposure to measurable business consequence

[Learn More](https://quantara.ai/cyber-loss-breach-analytics/)

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Exposure-Management.png)

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/pro1-1.png)

### Emerging Threats Analytics & Industry Benchmarking

Outside-in intelligence tailored to your industry and business profile

- Track active threat actors, attack vectors, and data loss trends
- Benchmark loss frequency and impact against peers and size tier
- Get real-time, research-driven insights on evolving threats and breaches

[Learn More](https://quantara.ai/threat-and-exposure-management/)

### Continuous Cyber Assessment

Automated, business-driven assessment aligned with ROI and continuous trust

- Evaluate controls across NIST, ISO, and CIS frameworks
- Apply Gartner CARTA principles for adaptive, continuous risk visibility
- Link maturity gains to ROI, RRI, and smarter investment planning

[Learn More](https://quantara.ai/continuous-assessment/)

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/img5.png)

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/img4.png)

### Executive Reporting & Decision Management

Automated, customizable reporting aligned to every risk committee — designed to go beyond manual reporting

- Use persona-based, automated templates for CISO, CFO, Board, and other cyber or business leaders
- Customize dashboards and widgets by committee or governance need
- Deliver faster, consistent, and defensible insights impossible with manual methods

[Learn More](https://quantara.ai/executive-reporting/)

## Integrations

# How it Works?

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/HowItsWorks.png)

![](https://quantara.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/HowItsWorks-mobile.png)

[See your Data in Quantara AI](https://www.crc.apps.quantara.ai/signup)

# Solutions

One Platform. Every Persona. Measurable Results.

### CISOs | CFOs | CEOs | Boards

- Quantify cyber risk in dollars and ROI with automated, board-ready reports
- Prove program value and optimize spend on controls and cyber insurance
- Bridge CISO–CFO alignment with clear, defensible financial narratives

[Learn more](https://wbtest.quantara.ai/chief-information-security-officer-ciso/)

### CROs | Risk & GRC Teams

- Enable always-on CRQ aligned to ERM, SEC, and leading frameworks (NIST, ISO)
- Run materiality and loss-impact scenarios to track financial exposure
- Establish risk appetite aligned to industry benchmarks

[Learn more](https://wbtest.quantara.ai/vulnerability-management/)

### SecOps | Compliance Teams

- Identify and fix the top 1% of exposures driving enterprise risk
- Map vulnerabilities, control gaps & threats to business impact instantly
- Replace spreadsheets with automated, continuous control maturity insights

[Learn more](https://wbtest.quantara.ai/governance-risk-compliance-grc/)

# Customer Outcomes

company.name: Quantara AI
company.about:
Quantara AI is a cutting-edge, automated cybersecurity risk-intelligence platform that enables enterprises across industries to Identify, Quantify, Prioritize, and Manage cyber risks across cloud, on-prem, OT, and SaaS environments.

We empower organizations to:
-Identify the top 1% of exposures that drive the most enterprise risk
-Quantify cyber risk in financial terms, proving measurable Risk Reduction & ROI (RRI)
-Prioritize mitigations aligned to business impact for smarter investments
-Manage outcomes with automated reporting, continuous compliance, and real-time analytics

The platform delivers insights tailored to boards, CISOs, CFOs, and risk leaders, translating cyber exposure into actionable business intelligence. With Cyber Risk Quantification (CRQ), business-aligned exposure and threat management, compliance enablement, automated executive reporting, and industry breach analytics customized to each client’s profile, Quantara AI proves the value of cybersecurity in business terms.

Key Outcomes:
-Clear business impact – Risk translated into financial and operational metrics for leadership
-Full visibility, faster action – Unified view across digital environments with adaptive, rapid integration
-Actionable results at scale – Prioritized mitigations, automated reporting, and governance with 10× faster insights at 40–75% lower cost

Quantara AI transforms cybersecurity into measurable business performance—helping enterprises make smarter decisions, maximize ROI, and strengthen resilience. Learn more at www.quantara.ai

references.title: Quantara AI Launches Industry's First Persistent AI-Powered Cyber Risk Solution
references.snippet:
BOISE, Idaho, Feb. 17, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Quantara AI today announced the general availability of its AI-powered Cyber Risk Intelligence platform, purpose-built to help enterprises continuously identify and quantify the small fraction of cyber exposures that account for the majority of financial risk.
[...]
Unlike traditional, point-in-time cyber risk assessments, Quantara AI delivers a persistent, always-on approach to Cyber Risk Quantification (CRQ), unifying threat intelligence, business context, and financial impact into a single system of record for CISOs, executives, and boards.
[...]
Traditional manual risk modeling has left enterprises trapped in a cycle of "static snapshots", point-in-time
[...]
that are outdated the moment they are finalized. Industry analysts, including IDC, predict a fundamental shift toward automated, predictive intelligence. By 2028, IDC estimates that 40% of enterprises
[...]
adopt AI-powered cyber risk quantification (CRQ) platforms to translate security metrics into financial exposure for budgeting and investment decisions.
[...]
Quantara AI is the first platform designed to continuously unify threat intelligence, industry losses, business context, and financial risk modeling in a single, always-on system. The platform focuses on three core capabilities:
[...]
- Continuous Threat and Business ContextQuantara AI correlates near real-time threat activity with the specific applications, data, and business processes at risk, incorporating adversary behavior and attack paths to surface the top 1% of exposures most likely to drive material loss.
[...]
- Quantified Risk and Mitigation ImpactUsing probabilistic modeling, the platform translates cyber exposure into financial loss estimates and Value at Risk (VaR), while quantifying Risk Reduction Impact (RRI) to show how security investments reduce risk per dollar spent.
[...]
- Executive-Ready AI OutcomesQuantara AI delivers board-ready reporting, natural-language risk insights, and automated mappings to regulatory and disclosure requirements, enabling faster, clearer, and more defensible cyber risk decisions.
[...]
By providing real-time persistent analysis, Quantara AI helps the enterprise make faster, smarter security and risk decisions. The solution is designed to phase out obsolete manual processes, including:
[...]
- Consultant-led, manual CRQ and spreadsheet-based risk modeling.
- Legacy GRC platforms that lack live threat and telemetry integration.
- Static Vulnerability Management that lacks business-criticality context.
- Manual Board Reporting that takes weeks of human effort to compile.
[...]
Quantara AI is an AI-powered cyber risk intelligence company. By delivering persistent, integrated risk analysis, Quantara AI enables organizations to identify, quantify, and prioritize material cyber risk in near real-time, aligning security spend with actual business outcomes.

references.title: Continuous Cyber Assessment | Quantara AI
references.snippet:
Continuous Cyber Assessment | Quantara AI 

# Quantify Control Maturity. Automate Compliance. Prove ROI.

Quantara AI automates cybersecurity assessments across frameworks like NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and NIST 800-53 — quantifying how control maturity impacts enterprise risk and ROI, continuously.

Start Cyber Assessment

Schedule Demo

# Key Features

01

Automated Control Mapping

Ingests cyber and business data, aligning it to NIST, ISO, and other frameworks in real time.

02

Quantified Maturity & VaR

Measures maturity across business units and shows the dollar impact of weak controls.

03

Dynamic Slicing & Insights

View maturity, risk reduction, and ROI by business unit, geography, or business applications.

04

Actionable Mitigation Roadmap

Recommends prioritized control improvements with measurable ROI and risk reduction metrics.

# How Quantara AI Powers It

- Ingest & AlignPulls structured and unstructured cyber data from across your stack and maps it automatically to compliance frameworks.
- Quantify ImpactCalculates Value at Risk (VaR) for each control gap, showing its financial impact on business units and processes.
- Benchmark MaturityCompares control effectiveness against framework baselines and peer performance.
- Recommend & JustifyProvides remediation guidance and ROI projections for each recommended improvement.

## integrations

# Use Cases & Benefits

- CISOs & Compliance TeamsReplace manual assessments with continuous, data-based assessments.
- CROs & Risk Officers Link control maturity to risk exposure, loss potential, and ERM alignment.
- CFOs & Executives Quantify ROI from compliance investments and track measurable risk reduction.
- Measured Outcomes Automate control assessments, reduce audit effort by 60%, and achieve real-time framework alignment in under 14 days.

# Never Rely on Point-in-Time Assessments Again.

Quantify maturity, reduce compliance cost, and prove ROI — automatically.

Best Mobile Banking App for Customer Satisfaction among Regional Banks.1

It’s easy with Fifth Third [online and mobile banking](https://53.com/content/fifth-third/en/personal-banking/bank/online-mobile-banking.html?omid=www:hp:onlinemobilebanking:app:). With our mobile app2, you can check balances, transfer money, deposit checks and more. It’s like having your own personal branch right inside your pocket!

company.name: Fifth Third Bank
company.about:
At Fifth Third Bank, everything we do is rooted in our purpose: to improve the lives of our customers and the well-being of our communities. Since our founding in 1858, we’ve been committed to creating a better financial experience by empowering our customers and clients to achieve what matters most.

Our unified strength is grounded in the individual passion and diversity of more than 20,000 employees who work collaboratively to deliver a better tomorrow to everyone we serve. We offer a strong culture, opportunities for growth 401k match, wellness options, comprehensive insurance plans and additional resources you need to build a lasting and rewarding career path here. 

Headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, we are among the largest money managers in the Midwest.

We operate four main businesses—Commercial Banking, Branch Banking, Consumer Lending, and Wealth & Asset Management—and a network of financial centers in Ohio, Kentucky, Indiana, Michigan, Illinois, Florida, Tennessee, West Virginia, Georgia, North Carolina and South Carolina. Consumers also have access to approximately 54,000 Fifth Third fee-free ATMs across the United States. 

Fifth Third Bancorp is a diversified financial services company and is the indirect parent company of Fifth Third Bank, National Association, a federally chartered institution.

Explore Fifth Third career opportunities at: https://www.53.com/content/fifth-third/en/careers.html 

Fifth Third Bank, N.A., Member FDIC. Fifth Third Bank is proud to be an affirmative action/equal opportunity employer. M/F/D/V

Fifth Third Bank uses AI in several production applications. 

*   **Jeanie Chatbot:** The chatbot uses traditional AI to manage customer inquiries (Tearsheet, 2025). It shortens account opening times, improves employee retention and customer satisfaction, generates annual savings, and reduces calls requiring a live agent (Emerj, 2025).

*   **Customer Sentiment Analysis:** Fifth Third uses NICE Enlighten AI and Nexidia Analytics to measure and improve customer experience at scale, with sentiment scoring as the centerpiece (NICE, 2022; Emerj, 2025). They analyze customer interactions to improve agent performance (Emerj, 2025). The bank analyzed over 15.7 million interactions involving 2,300 agents (Emerj, 2025).

*   **AI for Regulatory Compliance:** AI is used to prepare for bank exams (American Banker, 2026).

*   **Cost Reduction:** AI is used for cost savings (Bloomberg, 2026).

Barclays Group corporate website | Barclays

# Q1 2026 Results

Barclays announced its Q1 2026 Results on Tuesday 28 April. \r\n"}}" id="123">

Barclays announced its Q1 2026 Results on Tuesday 28 April.

## Our Sustainability insights

![](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section/sectionItem/sectionItem/grid_copy_copy/item_1734358502818_c/gridItem/tile/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.jpeg/1777029948004/hampton-court-palace-1080-1080.jpeg)

### In the Field: Mixergy at Hampton Court Palace

Historic buildings have limited retrofit options, as energy efficiency upgrades must be delivered without altering protected features. In this episode, Hampton Court Palace shows how they are working Mixergy to decarbonise their hot water supply. \n"}}" id="text-7300fce728">

Historic buildings have limited retrofit options, as energy efficiency upgrades must be delivered without altering protected features. In this episode, Hampton Court Palace shows how they are working Mixergy to decarbonise their hot water supply.

![Man looking at pylon](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section/sectionItem/sectionItem/grid_copy_copy/item_1727358903369_c/gridItem/tile_copy/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.jpeg/1777030095712/pylon-in-winter-grey-square.jpeg)

### Investing in the energy transition in a fragmented world

As geopolitical fragmentation intensifies, energy investment is increasingly focused on resilience. Barclays’ report ‘Transition Realism: Financing Energy Systems’ explores this shift and its intersection with the energy transition. \n"}}" id="text-5f700ed494">

As geopolitical fragmentation intensifies, energy investment is increasingly focused on resilience. Barclays’ report ‘Transition Realism: Financing Energy Systems’ explores this shift and its intersection with the energy transition.

![Underwater turbines for tidal energy.](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section/sectionItem/sectionItem/grid_copy_copy/item_1727358900601_c/gridItem/tile_copy_copy_copy_/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.jpeg/1777030176173/under-water-turbines-square.jpeg)

### Unlocking regional strengths to reinforce the UK’s climate tech leadership

Barclays calls on government to support regional climate tech clusters with better access to capital, accelerators and AI capability - turning regional strengths into national growth engines. \n"}}" id="text-528d531e1f">

Barclays calls on government to support regional climate tech clusters with better access to capital, accelerators and AI capability - turning regional strengths into national growth engines.

## Our UK unlocked insights

![Two children sat at a window as a plane takes off ](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section_719841816_co/grid_copy_copy_copy_/item_1722001186098_c/gridItem/tile_2026702043/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.png/1776931967853/travel-2026-square.png)

### Travel in 2026: A resilient industry with rewritten assumptions

Simon Atkinson, Head of Travel, Barclays Corporate Banking, analyses 2026’s key travel trends, and explores how the industry is managing the impact of the conflict in the Middle East.  \n"}}" id="text-151d1117a3">

Simon Atkinson, Head of Travel, Barclays Corporate Banking, analyses 2026’s key travel trends, and explores how the industry is managing the impact of the conflict in the Middle East.

![A man sits at a desk thinking](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section_719841816_co/grid_copy_copy_copy_/item_1722001186098_c_1985449787/gridItem/tile_2026702043/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.png/1777538706034/sasha-holding-back-square.png)

### What’s holding you back from investing?

As Barclays launches its Investment Readiness Index, which is designed to benchmark the UK’s investment culture ahead of a national Invest for the Future campaign, Sasha Wiggins, Chair of the Invest for the Future campaign and CEO of Barclays Private Bank & Wealth Management explains why. \n"}}" id="text-81b2c5f660">

As Barclays launches its Investment Readiness Index, which is designed to benchmark the UK’s investment culture ahead of a national Invest for the Future campaign, Sasha Wiggins, Chair of the Invest for the Future campaign and CEO of Barclays Private Bank & Wealth Management explains why.

![A smartly dressed man in the back of the car on his mobile phone](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section_719841816_co/grid_copy_copy_copy_/item_1722867725416_c/gridItem/tile_copy_copy/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.png/1777538937819/entrepreneurs-uku-square.png)

### Banking today’s entrepreneurs: Why old-fashioned rules still prevail in a changing world

Matthew Townsend, Director and leader of the High Growth Team at Barclays Private Bank and Wealth Management, gives his insight on what it’s like to bank the titans of tomorrow. \n"}}" id="text-6fcfc42065">

Matthew Townsend, Director and leader of the High Growth Team at Barclays Private Bank and Wealth Management, gives his insight on what it’s like to bank the titans of tomorrow.

## About Barclays

![Main at start of solo running race](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section_1253394739_c/section_item/sectionItem/grid_copy_1921361653/item_1713961429991/gridItem/tile_copy_755004660_/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.jpeg/1726139866049/our-strategy-1-1.jpeg)

### Our Strategy

We have a clear plan to improve our operational and financial performance, and improve total shareholder returns. To do so, over the next three years we will make Barclays Simpler, Better and More balanced. \n"}}" id="text-40bcde1a23">

We have a clear plan to improve our operational and financial performance, and improve total shareholder returns. To do so, over the next three years we will make Barclays Simpler, Better and More balanced.

### Our Structure

We are a diversified bank with comprehensive UK consumer, corporate and wealth and private banking franchises, a leading investment bank and a strong, specialist US consumer bank. \n"}}" id="text-68a52aa0b0">

We are a diversified bank with comprehensive UK consumer, corporate and wealth and private banking franchises, a leading investment bank and a strong, specialist US consumer bank.

### Addressing climate change

We have a clear strategy to meet our ambition to be a net zero bank by 2050: by achieving net zero operations, reducing our financed emissions and financing the transition. \n"}}" id="text-51bd09ccff">

We have a clear strategy to meet our ambition to be a net zero bank by 2050: by achieving net zero operations, reducing our financed emissions and financing the transition.

![Image of military helicopter landing](https://barclays.com/_jcr_content/root/body/content/section_1253394739_c/section_item/sectionItem/grid_copy_1921361653/item_1713961434598/gridItem/tile_copy_copy_42101/multimedia.coreimg.80.1440.jpeg/1727356615073/helicopter-1-1.jpeg)

With increasing geopolitical threats, the provision of financial services to the defence sector is increasingly important.  Find out more about the work we do. \n"}}" id="text-549fa9958a">

With AI-powered humanoid robots set to transform the workforce, our Research analysts unpack the investment themes driving AI’s next, more physical era. \n"}}" id="text-51defb7d4c">

company.name: Barclays Private Bank
company.about:
Barclays Private Bank provides comprehensive specialist investment, banking, lending and wealth advisory services to the world’s most influential people and their families in the primary global wealth hubs and corridors. 
 
With 330 years of experience, we surround clients with a team of trusted advisers and specialists who draw on the expertise they need to connect clients to insights and opportunities from across the Barclays Group. 

Based on our deep understanding of them, their families and businesses, we tailor opportunities to support our clients’ unique ambitions enabling them to invest in the future, and influence tomorrow with confidence.

For all client enquiries please contact us via: https://privatebank.barclays.com/contact-us/

Important Information:

Barclays offers private banking products and services to its clients through Barclays Bank PLC and its subsidiary companies. Barclays Bank PLC is registered in England and authorised by the Prudential Regulation Authority and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority and the Prudential Regulation Authority. Registered No. 1026167. Registered Office: 1 Churchill Place, London E14 5HP.

The internal company profile lists its industry as Financial Services and describes its core offerings as banking, lending, and wealth advisory, with no mention of AI infrastructure, inference, or AI‑native products. Therefore, Barclays meets the criterion of an enterprise company whose primary business is not AI‑related.

These sources provide clear evidence of production‑grade AI/ML inference in Barclays’ operations.

man at PC screen
# The General Data Protection Regulation
We’re committed to keeping your personal data safe.
## Our continued commitment to you
Our focus on respecting your privacy and safeguarding personal data remains as strong as ever. We’ve updated our privacy notices to reflect the new and strengthened rights in relation to your personal data, and the legal grounds for using it.
We’re committed to giving you control and flexibility. If we’re required by law to share your data with other organisations, such as the government, we do so securely and we don’t share more than we need to.
Whether it’s to deliver products and services to you and others, or to follow legal requirements, we’ll always be clear and open with you about how and why we’re using your data. And if you need more detail, we’ll always keep our privacy notices where you can find them easily.
When we ask other companies to process data on our behalf, we’ll always make sure they follow similarly high standards.
We’re committed to keeping your data safe and will only retain it for as long as necessary, before we dispose of it safely.
Read our Privacy Policy

## Important information
